---
title: "Social determinants of health and epigenetic clocks: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of 140 studies "
format: html
editor: source
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r packages-and-functions}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
#| 
# load packages and functions
library(tidyverse)
library(effectsize)
library(esc)
library(metafor)
library(gridExtra)
library(ggtext)
library(maps)

# initialize functions for variance distribution and heterogeneity analysis
variance_distribution <- function(dataset,output) {
  # function to determine how the total variance is distributed over the three levels 
  # of the meta-analytic model - returns percentages for all 3 levels
  
  n <- length(dataset$z_variance)
  list.inverse.variances <- 1 / (dataset$z_variance)
  sum.inverse.variances <- sum(list.inverse.variances)
  squared.sum.inverse.variances <- (sum.inverse.variances) ^ 2
  list.inverse.variances.square <- 1 / (dataset$z_variance^2)
  sum.inverse.variances.square <- sum(list.inverse.variances.square)
  numerator <- (n - 1) * sum.inverse.variances
  denominator <- squared.sum.inverse.variances - sum.inverse.variances.square
  estimated.sampling.variance <- numerator / denominator
  I2_1 <- (estimated.sampling.variance) / (output$sigma2[1]
                                           + output$sigma2[2] + estimated.sampling.variance)
  I2_2 <- (output$sigma2[1]) / (output$sigma2[1]
                                       + output$sigma2[2] + estimated.sampling.variance)
  I2_3 <- (output$sigma2[2]) / (output$sigma2[1]
                                       + output$sigma2[2] + estimated.sampling.variance)
  amountvariancelevel1 <- round(I2_1 * 100,2)
  amountvariancelevel2 <- round(I2_2 * 100,2)
  amountvariancelevel3 <- round(I2_3 * 100,2)
  
  return(c(amountvariancelevel1, amountvariancelevel2, amountvariancelevel3)) 
}

heterogeneity_analysis <- function(dataset, output) {
  # function to determine if there is heterogeneity in levels 2 and 3 - returns the p values of anovas 
  
  # Build a two-level model without within-study variance
  # Perform a likelihood-ratio-test to determine the significance of the between-study variance
  modelnovar2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID),
                        sigma2=c(0,NA), data=dataset)
  res2 <- anova(output,modelnovar2)
  
  # Build a two-level model without between-study variance;
  # Perform a likelihood-ratio-test to determine the significance of the between-study variance.
  modelnovar3 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID),
                        sigma2=c(NA,0), data=dataset)
  res3 <- anova(output,modelnovar3)
  
  return(c(res2$pval, res3$pval))
}
```

# Initialize and Organize Data

This chunk loads and organizes data, converts all effect sizes to Pearson's r then to Fisher's z and creates new columns to group data.

```{r load-and-finalize-data}
#| echo: false
#| output: false

# FILL OUT MISSING VALUES IN THE CODING SCHEME

#load data and create the dummy variables
data <- read.csv("final_dataset.csv") %>%
  rename(Sex_female_percent = Sex_female) %>% 
  add_column(DNAm_gen1 = 0, .after = "DNAm_gen") %>%
  add_column(DNAm_gen2 = 0, .after = "DNAm_gen1") %>%
  add_column(DNAm_gen3 = 0, .after = "DNAm_gen2") %>%
  add_column(DNAm_horvath1 = 0, .after = "DNAm_measure") %>%
  add_column(DNAm_hannum = 0, .after = "DNAm_horvath1") %>%
  add_column(DNAm_horvath2 = 0, .after = "DNAm_hannum") %>%
  add_column(DNAm_pedbe = 0, .after = "DNAm_horvath2") %>%
  add_column(DNAm_zhang = 0, .after = "DNAm_pedbe") %>%
  add_column(DNAm_phenoage = 0, .after = "DNAm_zhang") %>%
  add_column(DNAm_grimage = 0, .after = "DNAm_phenoage") %>%
  add_column(DNAm_grimage2 = 0, .after = "DNAm_grimage") %>%
  add_column(DNAm_dunedinpoam = 0, .after = "DNAm_grimage2") %>%
  add_column(DNAm_dunedinpace = 0, .after = "DNAm_dunedinpoam") %>%
  add_column(Tissue_blood_ven = 0, .after = "Tissue") %>%
  add_column(Tissue_blood_cord = 0, .after = "Tissue_blood_ven") %>%
  add_column(Tissue_blood_spot = 0, .after = "Tissue_blood_cord") %>%
  add_column(Tissue_buccal = 0, .after = "Tissue_blood_spot") %>%
  add_column(Tissue_saliva = 0, .after = "Tissue_buccal") %>%
  add_column(Array_epic = 0, .after = "Array") %>%
  add_column(Array_450k = 0, .after = "Array_epic") %>%
  add_column(Array_epic_450k = 0, .after = "Array_450k") %>%
  add_column(Array_na = 0, .after = "Array_epic_450k") %>%
  add_column(Cell_comp_not_acc = 0, .after = "Cell_comp") %>%
  add_column(Age_SES_categorical_prenatal = 0, .after = "Age_SES_categorical") %>%
  add_column(Age_SES_categorical_earlychild = 0, .after = "Age_SES_categorical_prenatal") %>%
  add_column(Age_SES_categorical_latechild = 0, .after = "Age_SES_categorical_earlychild") %>%
  add_column(Age_SES_categorical_adol = 0, .after = "Age_SES_categorical_latechild") %>%
  add_column(Age_SES_categorical_youngadult = 0, .after = "Age_SES_categorical_adol") %>%
  add_column(Age_SES_categorical_midadult = 0, .after = "Age_SES_categorical_youngadult") %>%
  add_column(Age_SES_categorical_oldadult = 0, .after = "Age_SES_categorical_midadult") %>%
  add_column(Age_SES_categorical_child_unspecified = 0, .after = "Age_SES_categorical_oldadult") %>%
  add_column(Age_DNAm_prenatal = 0, .after = "Age_DNAm_categ") %>%
  add_column(Age_DNAm_earlychild = 0, .after = "Age_DNAm_prenatal") %>%
  add_column(Age_DNAm_latechild = 0, .after = "Age_DNAm_earlychild") %>%
  add_column(Age_DNAm_adol = 0, .after = "Age_DNAm_latechild") %>%
  add_column(Age_DNAm_youngadult = 0, .after = "Age_DNAm_adol") %>%
  add_column(Age_DNAm_midadult = 0, .after = "Age_DNAm_youngadult") %>%
  add_column(Age_DNAm_oldadult = 0, .after = "Age_DNAm_midadult") %>%
  add_column(Sex_male = 0, .after = "Sex") %>%
  add_column(Sex_female = 0, .after = "Sex_male") %>%
  add_column(Sex_mixed = 0, .after = "Sex_female") %>%
  add_column(Race_category = 0, .after = "Target_Group") %>%
  add_column(Race_category_black = 0, .after = "Race_category") %>%
  add_column(Race_category_latinx = 0, .after = "Race_category_black") %>%
  add_column(Race_category_nonwhite = 0, .after = "Race_category_latinx") %>%
  add_column(Race_category_asian = 0, .after = "Race_category_nonwhite") %>%
  add_column(Race_category_other = 0, .after = "Race_category_asian") %>%
  add_column(Race_category_nblackother = 0, .after = "Race_category_other") %>%
  add_column(Race_category_nblacknlatinxother = 0, .after = "Race_category_nblackother") %>%
  add_column(Race_category_latinxwhite = 0, .after = "Race_category_nblacknlatinxother") %>%
  add_column(Covariates_num = 0, .after = "Covariates") %>%
  add_column(Covariates_lessthan_equal5 = 0, .after = "Covariates_num") %>%
  add_column(Covariates_morethan5 = 0, .after = "Covariates_lessthan_equal5") %>%
  add_column(Covariates_bmi = 0, .after = "Covariates_morethan5") %>%
  add_column(Covariates_no_bmi = 0, .after = "Covariates_bmi") %>%
  add_column(Covariates_smoking = 0, .after = "Covariates_no_bmi") %>%
  add_column(Covariates_no_smoking = 0, .after = "Covariates_smoking") 


data$Fisher_z <- 0; data$z_variance <- 0; 



for (n in 1:nrow(data)){
  
  ### add number of covariates 
  if (length(strsplit(data$Covariates[n], " ")[[1]]) > 1) {
    cov_num <- str_remove(data$Covariates[n], "\\(")
    data$Covariates_num[n] <- as.numeric(sub(" .*", "",cov_num))
  }
  
  
  ### assign dummy variables
  
  #covariate number > 5
  if (data$Covariates_num[n] > 5) {data$Covariates_morethan5[n] <- 1}
  else {data$Covariates_lessthan_equal5[n] <- 1}
  
  #covariate - controlled for bmi
  if (grepl("smok",data$Covariates[n])) {data$Covariates_smoking[n] <- 1}
  else {data$Covariates_no_smoking[n] <- 1}
  
  #covariate - controlled for smoking
  if (grepl("bmi",data$Covariates[n]) || grepl("BMI",data$Covariates[n])) {data$Covariates_bmi[n] <- 1}
  else {data$Covariates_no_bmi[n] <- 1}
  
  #dnam generation
  if (is.na(data$DNAm_gen[n]) == 0){
  if (data$DNAm_gen[n] == 1) {data$DNAm_gen1[n] <- 1}
  else if (data$DNAm_gen[n] == 2) {data$DNAm_gen2[n] <- 1}
  else if (data$DNAm_gen[n] == 3) {data$DNAm_gen3[n] <- 1}}
  
  #dnam measure
  if (grepl("Horvath1", data$DNAm_measure[n])) {data$DNAm_horvath1[n] <- 1}
  else if (grepl("Hannum", data$DNAm_measure[n])) {data$DNAm_hannum[n] <- 1}
  else if (grepl("Horvath2", data$DNAm_measure[n])) {data$DNAm_horvath2[n] <- 1}
  else if (grepl("PedBE", data$DNAm_measure[n])) {data$DNAm_pedbe[n] <- 1}
  else if (grepl("Zhang", data$DNAm_measure[n])) {data$DNAm_zhang[n] <- 1}
  else if (grepl("PhenoAge", data$DNAm_measure[n])) {data$DNAm_phenoage[n] <- 1}
  else if (grepl("GrimAge2", data$DNAm_measure[n])) {data$DNAm_grimage2[n] <- 1}
  else if (grepl("GrimAge", data$DNAm_measure[n])) {data$DNAm_grimage[n] <- 1}
  else if (grepl("DunedinPoAm", data$DNAm_measure[n])) {data$DNAm_dunedinpoam[n] <- 1}
  else if (grepl("DunedinPACE", data$DNAm_measure[n])) {data$DNAm_dunedinpace[n] <- 1}
  
  #tissue
  if (data$Tissue[n] == 1) {data$Tissue_blood_ven[n] <- 1; data$Tissue[n] <- "venous_blood"}
  else if (data$Tissue[n] == 2) {data$Tissue_blood_cord[n] <- 1; data$Tissue[n] <- "cord_blood"}
  else if (data$Tissue[n] == 3) {data$Tissue_blood_spot[n] <- 1; data$Tissue[n] <- "blood_spot"}
  else if (data$Tissue[n] == 4) {data$Tissue_buccal[n] <- 1; data$Tissue[n] <- "buccal"}
  else if (data$Tissue[n] == 5) {data$Tissue_saliva[n] <- 1; data$Tissue[n] <- "saliva"}
  

  #array
  if (data$Array[n] == 1) {data$Array_epic[n] <- 1; data$Array[n] <- "EPIC"}
  else if (data$Array[n] == 2) {data$Array_450k[n] <- 1; data$Array[n] <- "450k"}
  else if (data$Array[n] == 3) {data$Array_epic_450k[n] <- 1; data$Array[n] <- "EPIC&450k"}
  else if (data$Array[n] == 0) {data$Array_na[n] <- 1; data$Array[n] <- "N/A"}
  
  #cell composition
  if (data$Cell_comp[n] == 0) {data$Cell_comp_not_acc[n] <- 1}
  
  #sex 
  if (data$Sex[n] == 0) {data$Sex_male[n] <- 1; data$Sex[n] <- "male"}
  else if (data$Sex[n] == 1) {data$Sex_female[n] <- 1; data$Sex[n] <- "female"}
  else if (data$Sex[n] == 2) {data$Sex_mixed[n] <- 1; data$Sex[n] <- "mixed"}
  
  #SES timing (categorical)
  if (is.na(data$Age_SES_categorical[n])) {}
  else if (data$Age_SES_categorical[n] == 1) {data$Age_SES_categorical_prenatal[n] <- 1}
  else if (data$Age_SES_categorical[n] == 2) {data$Age_SES_categorical_earlychild[n] <- 1}
  else  if (data$Age_SES_categorical[n] == 3) {data$Age_SES_categorical_latechild[n] <- 1}
  else  if (data$Age_SES_categorical[n] == 4) {data$Age_SES_categorical_adol[n] <- 1}
  else  if (data$Age_SES_categorical[n] == 5) {data$Age_SES_categorical_youngadult[n] <- 1}
  else  if (data$Age_SES_categorical[n] == 6) {data$Age_SES_categorical_midadult[n] <- 1}
  else  if (data$Age_SES_categorical[n] == 7) {data$Age_SES_categorical_oldadult[n] <- 1}
  else if (data$Age_SES_categorical[n] == 8) {data$Age_SES_categorical_child_unspecified[n] <- 1}
  
  #age (categorical)
  if (data$Age_DNAm_categ[n] == 1) {data$Age_DNAm_prenatal[n] <- 1}
  else  if (data$Age_DNAm_categ[n] == 2) {data$Age_DNAm_earlychild[n] <- 1}
  else  if (data$Age_DNAm_categ[n] == 3) {data$Age_DNAm_latechild[n] <- 1}
  else  if (data$Age_DNAm_categ[n] == 4) {data$Age_DNAm_adol[n] <- 1}
  else  if (data$Age_DNAm_categ[n] == 5) {data$Age_DNAm_youngadult[n] <- 1}
  else  if (data$Age_DNAm_categ[n] == 6) {data$Age_DNAm_midadult[n] <- 1}
  else  if (data$Age_DNAm_categ[n] == 7) {data$Age_DNAm_oldadult[n] <- 1}
  
  #race categories
  if (data$Reference_Group[n] == "Black" || data$Target_Group[n] == "Black") {data$Race_category[n] <- "Black"}
  else if (data$Reference_Group[n] == "Latinx" || data$Target_Group[n] == "Latinx") {data$Race_category[n] <- "Latinx"}

  if (data$Race_category[n] == "Black") {data$Race_category_black[n] <- 1}
  else if (data$Race_category[n] == "Latinx") {data$Race_category_latinx[n] <- 1}
  
  
  #change sd or se of BA measure if it is 0
  if (is.na(data$BA_sd[n]) == FALSE) {
    if (data$BA_sd[n] == 0) {data$BA_sd[n] <- 0.01}}
  
  if (is.na(data$BA_se[n]) == FALSE) {
    if (data$BA_se[n] == 0) {data$BA_se[n] <- 0.01}}
    
  #create effect size id
  data$Effect_Size_ID[n] <- n
  
  
  ### TRANSFORM EFFECT SIZES
  
  #initialize variables
  ref_n <- as.numeric(data$ref_n[n]) #sample size of reference group
  target_n <- as.numeric(data$target_n[n]) #sample size of comparison group
  d <- as.numeric(data$Cohen_d[n])
  beta <- as.numeric(data$standardized_beta[n])
  b <- as.numeric(data$unstandardized_b[n])
  sdoh_sd <- as.numeric(data$SDOH_sd[n]) 
  sdoh_se <- as.numeric(data$SDOH_se[n])
  ba_sd <- as.numeric(data$BA_sd[n])   
  ba_se <- as.numeric(data$BA_se[n])
  sample_size <- as.numeric(data$Effect_Size_n[n])
  spearman_rho <- as.numeric(data$spearman_rho[n])
  t <- as.numeric(data$t[n])
  df_error <- data$df_error[n]
  df_ttest <- data$df_ttest[n]

  #calculate sd from se, if sd isnt available  
  if (is.na(ba_sd) == TRUE) { #if sd of BA measure is not known
    ba_sd <- sqrt(sample_size)*ba_se}
  
  if (is.na(sdoh_sd) == TRUE) { #if sd of SDoH measure is not known
    sdoh_sd <- sqrt(sample_size)*sdoh_se}

  
  ## d to r
  if (is.na(d) == FALSE) {
    data$Pearson_r[n] <- d_to_r(d)
  }
  
  ## b to r
  if (is.na(b) == FALSE && (is.na(beta))) {
    
    #for categorical SDoH measures - calculate r directly
    if (data$SDOH_categorical[n] == 1 && (is.na(ba_sd) + is.na(ref_n) + is.na(target_n) == 0)) { 
      
      output <- esc_B(b, ba_sd, target_n, ref_n, es.type = "r") 
      data$Pearson_r[n] <- output[1]
      
    } #for continuous SDoH measures - calculate beta first, then convert it to r
    else if (data$SDOH_categorical[n] == 0 && (is.na(ba_sd) + is.na(sdoh_sd) == 0)) {
      
      #calculate beta from b
      beta <- (sdoh_sd/ba_sd)*b

  }}
    ## beta to r - based on the formula by Peterson and Brown, 2005
    if (is.na(beta) == FALSE) {
      if (abs(beta) <= 0.5) { # formula works if absolute value of beta is above 0.5
        if (beta < 0) {lambda <- 0}
        else if (beta >= 0) {lambda <- 1}
        
        data$Pearson_r[n] <- beta + 0.05*lambda}
      
      #if absolute beta is larger than 0.5, use a function from esc (only for two effect sizes)
      if (abs(beta) > 0.5 && (is.na(ref_n) + is.na(target_n) == 0)) {
        output <- esc_beta(beta, ba_sd, target_n, ref_n, es.type = "r")
        data$Pearson_r[n] <- output[1]}
    }
    
  ## spearman's rho to r
  if (is.na(spearman_rho) == FALSE) {
    data$Pearson_r[n] <- 2*sin(pi*spearman_rho/6)
  }
  
  ## t statistic to r for regression (1 study)
  if (is.na(t) == FALSE && is.na(df_error) == FALSE) {
    result <- t_to_r(t, df_error)
    data$Pearson_r[n] <- result$r
  }
    
    
  ## change direction of pearson's r if it is marked to be reverse coded
  if (is.na(data$Reverse_coding[n]) == FALSE &&  data$Reverse_coding[n] == 1) {
    data$Pearson_r[n] <- as.numeric(data$Pearson_r[n]) * -1
  }
    
  ## compute fisher's z from r
  if (is.na(data$Pearson_r[n]) == FALSE) {
    data$Fisher_z[n] <- convert_r2z(as.numeric(data$Pearson_r[n]))
    data$z_variance[n] <- 1/(sample_size-3)
  }
}

#separate data frame for change and bicor
change_data <- data %>%
  filter(change_measure != 0)

bicor_data <- data %>%
  filter(is.na(bicor) == FALSE)

#finalize dataset for ses and race separately
data_ses <- data %>%
  filter(change_measure == 0) %>%
  filter(is.na(bicor))  %>%
  filter(SDOH_category != "race")

data_race <- data %>%
  filter(change_measure == 0) %>%
  filter(is.na(bicor))  %>%
  filter(SDOH_category == "race") 
  

#label ses categories, like adulthood ses-dnam, childhood ses-adulthood dnam, childhood ses-dnam
data_ses$SES_time1 <- 0; data_ses$SES_time2 <- 0; data_ses$SES_time3 <- 0
data_ses$SES_adult <- 0; data_ses$SES_child <- 0; data_ses$DNAm_adult <- 0; data_ses$DNAm_child <- 0
data_race$DNAm_adult <- 0; data_race$DNAm_child <- 0

for (n in 1:nrow(data_ses)) {
  if (data_ses$Age_DNAm_categ[n] %in% c(5,6,7) && data_ses$Age_SES_categorical[n] %in% c(5,6,7)) {
    data_ses$SES_time[n] <- "1" # adult ses & dnam
    data_ses$SES_time1[n] <- 1
    data_ses$SES_adult[n] <- 1
    data_ses$DNAm_adult[n] <- 1
  } else if (data_ses$Age_DNAm_categ[n] %in% c(5,6,7) && data_ses$Age_SES_categorical[n] %in% c(1,2,3,4,8)) {
    data_ses$SES_time[n] <- "2" # child ses & adult dnam 
    data_ses$SES_time2[n] <- 1
    data_ses$SES_child[n] <- 1
    data_ses$DNAm_adult[n] <- 1
  } else if (data_ses$Age_DNAm_categ[n] %in% c(1,2,3,4) && data_ses$Age_SES_categorical[n] %in% c(1,2,3,4,8)) {
    data_ses$SES_time[n] <- "3" # child ses & dnam
    data_ses$SES_time3[n] <- 1
    data_ses$SES_child[n] <- 1
    data_ses$DNAm_child[n] <- 1
  }}

for (n in 1:nrow(data_race)) {
  if (data_race$Age_DNAm_categ[n] %in% c(5,6,7)) {
    data_race$DNAm_adult[n] <- 1
  } else if (data_race$Age_DNAm_categ[n] %in% c(1,2,3,4)) {
    data_race$DNAm_child[n] <- 1
}}
```


ONLY FOR SENSITIVITY ANALYSIS WITHOUT META-ANALYSES : I changed the data frame here and ran the analyses as usual to run the model without data from the three meta-analyses. Only run this chunk to perform the analysis without meta-analyses. Don't forget to change the name of the plot.

-- only 4 effect sizes from 2 cohorts (35,102) are removed, because cohort 49 only contributed bicor effect sizes, which are analyzed separately. Race is not included in any of the associations, so no need to analyze it.

```{r meta-analysis}
data_ses <- data_ses %>%
  filter(Study_ID %in% c(35,49,102) == FALSE)

```

ONLY FOR SENSITIVITY ANALYSIS WITHOUT PREPRINTS : I changed the data frame here and ran the analyses as usual to run the model without data from the three preprints (studies #81, #88 and #123). Only run this chunk to perform the analysis without preprints. Don't forget to change the name of the plot.

```{r preprints}
data_ses <- data_ses %>%
  filter(Study_ID %in% c(81,88,123) == FALSE)

data_race <- data_race %>%
  filter(Study_ID %in% c(81,88,123) == FALSE)
```

ONLY FOR SENSITIVTY ANALYSIS WITHOUT EFFECT SIZES WITH SMALL SAMPLE SIZES: Exclude effect sizes from generation and dnam-level analyses if they have sample sizes below 100. Only run this chunk to perform the analysis without preprints. Don't forget to change the name of the plot.

```{r low_n}
data_ses <- data_ses %>%
  filter(Effect_Size_n >= 100)

data_race <- data_race %>%
  filter(Effect_Size_n >= 100)
```

# SES

All analyses use the z distribution, as our effect size estimates are also fisher's z. Since it is the default of the function, it is not mentioned separately. When using the t distribution, the results change very minimally (e.g. 0.0001 change in confidence interval bounds).

This first 3 chunks of the code first performs the 3 level meta-analysis at the generation level, then at the dnam measure level, and finally creates a forest plot.

## Generations

```{r}
# associations per generation
# gen 1
gen1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

robust(gen1, cluster = data_ses$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

# gen 2
gen2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

robust(gen2, cluster = data_ses$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

# gen 3
gen3 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

robust(gen3, cluster = data_ses$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(data_ses,gen1) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses,gen1)
```

## DNAm Measures

```{r}
# associations of DNAm measures
## first gen
#horvath1
horvath1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

robust(horvath1, cluster = data_ses$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

#hannum
hannum <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

robust(hannum, cluster = data_ses$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

#horvath2
horvath2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

robust(horvath2, cluster = data_ses$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

#zhang
zhang <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

robust(zhang, cluster = data_ses$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

#pedbe
pedbe <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_horvath1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

robust(pedbe, cluster = data_ses$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

## second gen
#phenoage
phenoage <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

robust(phenoage, cluster = data_ses$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

#grimage
grimage <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

robust(grimage, cluster = data_ses$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

## third gen
#dunedinpoam
poam <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

robust(poam, cluster = data_ses$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

#dunedinpace
pace <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

robust(pace, cluster = data_ses$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for DNAm-level analyses (since the meta-analytic model is the same for all DNAm measures, running this once should be enough)
variance_distribution(data_ses,horvath1) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses,horvath1)
```

## Figure

```{r}
#get estimates, lower and higher cis for graphs (full dataset)
ests <- c(coef(gen1)[1], coef(horvath1)[1], coef(hannum)[1], coef(horvath2)[1], coef(zhang)[1], coef(pedbe)[1], 
          coef(gen2)[1], coef(phenoage)[1], coef(grimage)[1], coef(gen3)[1], coef(poam)[1], coef(pace)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1$ci.lb[1], horvath1$ci.lb[1], hannum$ci.lb[1], horvath2$ci.lb[1], zhang$ci.lb[1], pedbe$ci.lb[1],
            gen2$ci.lb[1], phenoage$ci.lb[1], grimage$ci.lb[1], gen3$ci.lb[1], poam$ci.lb[1], pace$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1$ci.ub[1], horvath1$ci.ub[1], hannum$ci.ub[1], horvath2$ci.ub[1], zhang$ci.ub[1], pedbe$ci.ub[1],
            gen2$ci.ub[1], phenoage$ci.ub[1], grimage$ci.ub[1], gen3$ci.ub[1], poam$ci.ub[1], pace$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1$pval[1], horvath1$pval[1], hannum$pval[1], horvath2$pval[1], zhang$pval[1], pedbe$pval[1],
            gen2$pval[1], phenoage$pval[1], grimage$pval[1], gen3$pval[1], poam$pval[1], pace$pval[1])
  
es_no <- c(sum(data_ses$DNAm_gen1), sum(data_ses$DNAm_horvath1), sum(data_ses$DNAm_hannum), 
           sum(data_ses$DNAm_horvath2), sum(data_ses$DNAm_zhang), sum(data_ses$DNAm_pedbe), 
           sum(data_ses$DNAm_gen2), sum(data_ses$DNAm_phenoage), sum(data_ses$DNAm_grimage),
           sum(data_ses$DNAm_gen3), sum(data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam), sum(data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpace))


# convert all z's to r
ests <- convert_z2r(ests)
lowcis <- convert_z2r(lowcis)
highcis <- convert_z2r(highcis)


# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Hannum", "Horvath Skin & Blood", "Zhang", "PedBE",
                   "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "3rd Generation", "DunedinPoAm", "DunedinPACE"),           # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no,                        # number of effect sizes
  p_val = round(pvals,2))

png("ses_all.png", width = 38, height = 25, units = "cm", res = 600)

par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))

# Create the forest plot for the summary effects

forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Pearson's r [95% CI])", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = cbind(summary_effects$ES_no, summary_effects$p_val), ilab.xpos = cbind(-0.3, 0.47),
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 2,
       cex.axis = 1.8,
       textpos = as.numeric(c(-0.6, 0.37)),
       xlim = c(-0.6,0.5),
       alim = c(-0.25,0.15))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits

# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 2.5, par("usr")[2], 3.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 3
rect(par("usr")[1], 5.5, par("usr")[2], 6.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 6
rect(par("usr")[1], 11.5, par("usr")[2], 12.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 12


text(-0.29, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

text(0.45, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "p-value", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

title("Association of Socioeconomic Status and DNAm Measures", line = 0, cex.main = 2.5)

# Add * significant results
for (n in 1:length(pvals)) {
  if (pvals[n] < 0.05) {
    mtext("*", side = 4, line = 1, cex = 3, las = 1, at = (length(pvals)-n+1))
  }
}
dev.off()
```

## Moderators

Next 9 chunks run moderator analyses for the 9 DNAm measures. Moderators are separated by "\## NAME". Only the moderator categories that have at least 5 effect sizes are included as intercepts - for example, in Horvath1 there's less than 5 effect sizes for cord blood, so, it is not treated as an intercept and its effect is not calculated. However, as it still has some effect sizes, it is included in the "mods =" section of other tissues, so that the moderator model is complete.

### Horvath1

```{r}
## ARRAY
#450k as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_epic + Array_epic_450k, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

#EPIC as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_450k + Array_epic_450k, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

#both as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_epic + Array_450k, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])


## TISSUE
#venous blood as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_spot + Tissue_blood_cord + Tissue_saliva + Tissue_buccal, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

#blood spot as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_ven + Tissue_blood_cord + Tissue_saliva + Tissue_buccal, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

#saliva as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_spot + Tissue_blood_cord + Tissue_blood_ven + Tissue_buccal, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

#buccal as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_spot + Tissue_blood_cord + Tissue_saliva + Tissue_blood_ven, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])


## CELL COMPOSITION
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])


## SEX
#female as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Sex_male + Sex_mixed, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

#mixed as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Sex_male + Sex_female, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

```

### Hannum

```{r}
## ARRAY
#450k as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_epic + Array_epic_450k + Array_na, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_hannum == 1,])

#EPIC as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_450k + Array_epic_450k + Array_na, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_hannum == 1,])


## TISSUE
#venous blood as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_spot + Tissue_blood_cord + Tissue_saliva, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_hannum == 1,])

#blood spot as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_ven + Tissue_blood_cord + Tissue_saliva, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_hannum == 1,])

#saliva as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_spot + Tissue_blood_cord + Tissue_blood_ven, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_hannum == 1,])



## CELL COMPOSITION
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_hannum == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_hannum == 1,])


## SEX
#female as reference
hannum_female <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Sex_male + Sex_mixed, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_hannum == 1,])

#mixed as reference
hannum_mixed <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Sex_male + Sex_female, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_hannum == 1,])

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_hannum == 1,],hannum_female) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_hannum == 1,],hannum_female)

```

### Horvath2

```{r}

## ARRAY

#450k as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_epic, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath2 == 1,])

#EPIC as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_450k, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath2 == 1,])


## TISSUE - NOT ENOUGH EFFECT SIZES (>5 only for venous blood)


## CELL COMPOSITION
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath2 == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath2 == 1,])


## SEX - ONLY MIXED CATEGORY


```

### Zhang

```{r}
## ARRAY
#450k as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_epic, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_zhang == 1,])

#EPIC as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_450k, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_zhang == 1,])


## TISSUE
#venous blood as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_spot, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_zhang == 1,])

#blood spot as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_ven, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_zhang == 1,])


## CELL COMPOSITION - ALL EFFECT SIZES ARE CORRECTED


## SEX
#female as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Sex_mixed, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_zhang == 1,])

#mixed as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Sex_female, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_zhang == 1,])


```

### PedBE

```{r}
## ARRAY - NOT ENOUGH EFFECT SIZES (only EPIC is >5)


## TISSUE - NOT ENOUGH EFFECT SIZES (only saliva is >5)


## CELL COMPOSITION - NOT ENOUGH EFFECT SIZES (only corrected is >5)


## SEX - ALL MIXED


```

### PhenoAge

```{r}
## ARRAY
#450k as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_epic + Array_epic_450k, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])

#EPIC as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_450k + Array_epic_450k, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])

#both as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_epic + Array_450k, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])


## TISSUE
#venous blood as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_spot + Tissue_saliva + Tissue_buccal, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])

#blood spot as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_ven + Tissue_saliva + Tissue_buccal, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])

#saliva as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_spot + Tissue_blood_ven + Tissue_buccal, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])



## CELL COMPOSITION
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])


## SEX
#female as reference
pheno_female <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Sex_mixed, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])

#mixed as reference
pheno_mixed <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Sex_female, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_phenoage == 1,],pheno_female) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_phenoage == 1,],pheno_female)

```

### GrimAge

```{r}
## ARRAY
#450k as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_epic + Array_epic_450k, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,])

#EPIC as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_450k + Array_epic_450k, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,])

#both as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_epic + Array_450k, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,])


## TISSUE
#venous blood as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_spot + Tissue_saliva + Tissue_buccal, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,])

#blood spot as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_ven + Tissue_saliva + Tissue_buccal, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,])

#saliva as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_spot + Tissue_blood_ven + Tissue_buccal, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,])



## CELL COMPOSITION
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,])


## SEX
#female as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Sex_mixed, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,])

#mixed as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Sex_female, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,])

```

### DunedinPoAm

```{r}
## ARRAY
#450k as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_epic + Array_epic_450k, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])

#EPIC as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_450k + Array_epic_450k, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])

#both as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_epic + Array_450k, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])


## TISSUE
#venous blood as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_spot + Tissue_saliva, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])

#blood spot as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_ven + Tissue_saliva, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])

#saliva as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_spot + Tissue_blood_ven, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])



## CELL COMPOSITION
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])


## SEX
#female as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Sex_mixed, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])

#mixed as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Sex_female, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])

```

### DunedinPACE

```{r}
## ARRAY
#450k as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_epic + Array_epic_450k, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpace == 1,])

#EPIC as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_450k + Array_epic_450k, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpace == 1,])


## TISSUE
#venous blood as reference - only venous blood has enough effect sizes, so comparisons not run
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_buccal + Tissue_saliva, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpace == 1,])



## CELL COMPOSITION
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpace == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpace == 1,])


## SEX
#mixed as reference - only mixed has enough effect sizes so comparisons not run
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Sex_female + Sex_male, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpace == 1,])

```

## SES times

This part of the code includes 3 sections for SES/DNAm time analyses - adulthood SES & adulthood DNAm, childhood SES & adulthood DNAm, childhood SES & childhood DNAm. All 3 sections do the exact same thing for the main SES analyses - first runs the analyses for generations, then for DNAm measures, and then plots it.

## Adulthood SES & Adulthood DNAm

### Generations

```{r}
# associations per generation
# gen 1
gen1_ses1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,])

# gen 2
gen2_ses1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,])

# gen 3
gen3_ses1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,])

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,],gen1_ses1) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,],gen1_ses1)
```

### DNAm Measures

```{r}
# associations of DNAm measures
## first gen
#horvath1
horvath1_ses1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,])

#hannum
hannum_ses1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,])

#horvath2
horvath2_ses1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,])

#zhang
zhang_ses1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_horvath1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,])

## second gen
#phenoage
phenoage_ses1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,])

#grimage
grimage_ses1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,])

## third gen
#dunedinpoam
poam_ses1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,])

#dunedinpace
pace_ses1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,])

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,],horvath1_ses1) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,],horvath1_ses1)
```

### Age on a granular level

```{r}

# Adults's DNAm - Adulthood SES : Testing whether SES - DNAm differed on a granular age level 

data_ses_adult <- data_ses %>%
  filter(SES_time == 1)

subset_data <- data_ses_adult[data_ses_adult$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1, ]
subset_data <- data_ses_adult[data_ses_adult$DNAm_grimage == 1, ]

#so GrimAge
table(subset_data$Age_DNAm_youngadult) #33
table(subset_data$Age_DNAm_midadult) #62
table(subset_data$Age_DNAm_oldadult) #51

#so DunedinPoAm
table(subset_data$Age_DNAm_youngadult) #9
table(subset_data$Age_DNAm_midadult) #26
table(subset_data$Age_DNAm_oldadult) #37  

#is the association between Adulthood SES and Adulthood DunedinPoam different across more granular level of age bins?

#young adult reference
#No Significant difference
granularPoAm <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Age_DNAm_midadult + Age_DNAm_oldadult, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), 
       data=data_ses_adult[data_ses_adult$DNAm_dunedinpoam ==1, ]) 

#Age_DNAm_midadult reference
#No Significant difference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Age_DNAm_youngadult + Age_DNAm_oldadult, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), 
       data=data_ses_adult[data_ses_adult$DNAm_dunedinpoam ==1, ]) 

#Age_DNAm_oldadult reference
#No Significant difference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Age_DNAm_youngadult + Age_DNAm_midadult, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), 
       data=data_ses_adult[data_ses_adult$DNAm_dunedinpoam ==1, ]) 

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(data_ses_adult[data_ses_adult$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,],granularPoAm) 

#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses_adult[data_ses_adult$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,],granularPoAm) 


#is the association between Adulthood SES and Adulthood grimage different across more granular level of age bins?
#young adult reference
#No Significant difference
granularGrim<- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Age_DNAm_midadult + Age_DNAm_oldadult, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), 
       data=data_ses_adult[data_ses_adult$DNAm_grimage ==1, ]) 

#Age_DNAm_midadult reference
#No Significant difference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Age_DNAm_youngadult + Age_DNAm_oldadult, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), 
       data=data_ses_adult[data_ses_adult$DNAm_grimage ==1, ]) 

#Age_DNAm_oldadult reference
#No Significant difference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Age_DNAm_youngadult + Age_DNAm_midadult, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), 
       data=data_ses_adult[data_ses_adult$DNAm_grimage ==1, ]) 


#is the association between Adulthood SES and Adulthood GrimAge moderated by dnam age (continuous)?
#not significant
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Age_DNAm_Mean, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), 
       data=data_ses_adult[data_ses_adult$DNAm_grimage ==1, ]) 


#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(data_ses_adult[data_ses_adult$DNAm_grimage == 1,],granularGrim) 

#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses_adult[data_ses_adult$DNAm_grimage == 1,],granularGrim) 



```

### Figure

```{r}
#get estimates, lower and higher cis for graphs (adulthood ses-adulthood dnam dataset)
ests <- c(coef(gen1_ses1)[1], coef(horvath1_ses1)[1], coef(hannum_ses1)[1], coef(horvath2_ses1)[1],
          coef(zhang_ses1)[1], coef(gen2_ses1)[1], coef(phenoage_ses1)[1],
          coef(grimage_ses1)[1], coef(gen3_ses1)[1], coef(poam_ses1)[1], coef(pace_ses1)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1_ses1$ci.lb[1], horvath1_ses1$ci.lb[1], hannum_ses1$ci.lb[1], horvath2_ses1$ci.lb[1], 
            zhang_ses1$ci.lb[1], gen2_ses1$ci.lb[1], phenoage_ses1$ci.lb[1], 
            grimage_ses1$ci.lb[1], gen3_ses1$ci.lb[1], poam_ses1$ci.lb[1], pace_ses1$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1_ses1$ci.ub[1], horvath1_ses1$ci.ub[1], hannum_ses1$ci.ub[1], horvath2_ses1$ci.ub[1], 
             zhang_ses1$ci.ub[1], gen2_ses1$ci.ub[1], phenoage_ses1$ci.ub[1], 
             grimage_ses1$ci.ub[1], gen3_ses1$ci.ub[1], poam_ses1$ci.ub[1], pace_ses1$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1_ses1$pval[1], horvath1_ses1$pval[1], hannum_ses1$pval[1], horvath2_ses1$pval[1], zhang_ses1$pval[1], 
           gen2_ses1$pval[1], phenoage_ses1$pval[1], grimage_ses1$pval[1], gen3_ses1$pval[1], 
           poam_ses1$pval[1], pace_ses1$pval[1])
  
es_no <- c(sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,]$DNAm_gen1), sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,]$DNAm_horvath1), 
           sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,]$DNAm_hannum),sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,]$DNAm_horvath2),
          sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,]$DNAm_zhang), sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,]$DNAm_gen2), 
          sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,]$DNAm_phenoage),sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,]$DNAm_grimage),
          sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,]$DNAm_gen3),sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,]$DNAm_dunedinpoam),
          sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time1 == 1,]$DNAm_dunedinpace))


# convert all z's to r
ests <- convert_z2r(ests)
lowcis <- convert_z2r(lowcis)
highcis <- convert_z2r(highcis)


# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Hannum", "Horvath Skin & Blood", "Zhang",
                   "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "3rd Generation", "DunedinPoAm", "DunedinPACE"),           # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no,                        # number of effect sizes
  p_val = round(pvals,2))

png("adult_ses_adult_dnam.png", width = 38, height = 25, units = "cm", res = 600)

par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))

# Create the forest plot for the summary effects

forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Pearson's r [95% CI])", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = cbind(summary_effects$ES_no, summary_effects$p_val), ilab.xpos = cbind(-0.3, 0.47),
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 2,
       cex.axis = 1.8,
       textpos = as.numeric(c(-0.6, 0.37)),
       xlim = c(-0.6,0.5),
       alim = c(-0.25,0.15))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits


# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 2.5, par("usr")[2], 3.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 3
rect(par("usr")[1], 5.5, par("usr")[2], 6.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 6
rect(par("usr")[1], 10.5, par("usr")[2], 11.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 11


text(-0.29, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

text(0.45, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "p-value", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

title("Association of Adulthood SES and DNAm Measures\nof Biological Aging Measured During Adulthood", line = 0, cex.main = 2.5)

# Add * significant results
for (n in 1:length(pvals)) {
  if (pvals[n] < 0.05) {
    mtext("*", side = 4, line = 1, cex = 3, las = 1, at = (length(pvals)-n+1))
  }
}

dev.off()
```


## Childhood SES & Adulthood DNAm

### Generations

```{r}
# associations per generation
# gen 1
gen1_ses2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,])

# gen 2
gen2_ses2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,])

# gen 3
gen3_ses2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,])

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,],gen1_ses2) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,],gen1_ses2)
```

### DNAm Measures

```{r}
# associations of DNAm measures
## first gen
#horvath1
horvath1_ses2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,])

#hannum
hannum_ses2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,])

#horvath2
horvath2_ses2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,])

#zhang
zhang_ses2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_horvath1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,])

## second gen
#phenoage
phenoage_ses2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,])

#grimage
grimage_ses2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,])

## third gen
#dunedinpoam
poam_ses2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,])

#dunedinpace
pace_ses2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,])

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,],horvath1_ses2) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,],horvath1_ses2)
```

### Figure

```{r}
#get estimates, lower and higher cis for graphs (childhood ses-adulthood dnam dataset)
ests <- c(coef(gen1_ses2)[1], coef(horvath1_ses2)[1], coef(hannum_ses2)[1], coef(horvath2_ses2)[1],
          coef(zhang_ses2)[1], coef(gen2_ses2)[1], coef(phenoage_ses2)[1],
          coef(grimage_ses2)[1], coef(gen3_ses2)[1], coef(poam_ses2)[1], coef(pace_ses2)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1_ses2$ci.lb[1], horvath1_ses2$ci.lb[1], hannum_ses2$ci.lb[1], horvath2_ses2$ci.lb[1], 
            zhang_ses2$ci.lb[1], gen2_ses2$ci.lb[1], phenoage_ses2$ci.lb[1], 
            grimage_ses2$ci.lb[1], gen3_ses2$ci.lb[1], poam_ses2$ci.lb[1], pace_ses2$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1_ses2$ci.ub[1], horvath1_ses2$ci.ub[1], hannum_ses2$ci.ub[1], horvath2_ses2$ci.ub[1], 
             zhang_ses2$ci.ub[1], gen2_ses2$ci.ub[1], phenoage_ses2$ci.ub[1], 
             grimage_ses2$ci.ub[1], gen3_ses2$ci.ub[1], poam_ses2$ci.ub[1], pace_ses2$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1_ses2$pval[1], horvath1_ses2$pval[1], hannum_ses2$pval[1], horvath2_ses2$pval[1], zhang_ses2$pval[1], 
           gen2_ses2$pval[1], phenoage_ses2$pval[1], grimage_ses2$pval[1], gen3_ses2$pval[1], 
           poam_ses2$pval[1], pace_ses2$pval[1])
  
es_no <- c(sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,]$DNAm_gen1), sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,]$DNAm_horvath1), 
           sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,]$DNAm_hannum),sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,]$DNAm_horvath2),
          sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,]$DNAm_zhang), sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,]$DNAm_gen2), 
          sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,]$DNAm_phenoage),sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,]$DNAm_grimage),
          sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,]$DNAm_gen3),sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,]$DNAm_dunedinpoam),
          sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time2 == 1,]$DNAm_dunedinpace))


# convert all z's to r
ests <- convert_z2r(ests)
lowcis <- convert_z2r(lowcis)
highcis <- convert_z2r(highcis)


# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Hannum", "Horvath Skin & Blood", "Zhang",
                   "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "3rd Generation", "DunedinPoAm", "DunedinPACE"),           # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no,                        # number of effect sizes
  p_val = round(pvals,2))

png("child_ses_adult_dnam.png", width = 38, height = 25, units = "cm", res = 600)

par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))

# Create the forest plot for the summary effects

forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Pearson's r [95% CI])", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = cbind(summary_effects$ES_no, summary_effects$p_val), ilab.xpos = cbind(-0.3, 0.47),
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 2,
       cex.axis = 1.8,
       textpos = as.numeric(c(-0.6, 0.37)),
       xlim = c(-0.6,0.5),
       alim = c(-0.25,0.15))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits


# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 2.5, par("usr")[2], 3.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 3
rect(par("usr")[1], 5.5, par("usr")[2], 6.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 6
rect(par("usr")[1], 10.5, par("usr")[2], 11.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 12

text(-0.29, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

text(0.45, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "p-value", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

title("Association of Childhood SES and DNAm Measures\nof Biological Aging Measured During Adulthood", line = 0, cex.main = 2.5)

# Add * significant results
for (n in 1:length(pvals)) {
  if (pvals[n] < 0.05) {
    mtext("*", side = 4, line = 1, cex = 3, las = 1, at = (length(pvals)-n+1))
  }
}

dev.off()
```

## Childhood SES & Childhood DNAm

### Generations

```{r}
# associations per generation
# gen 1
gen1_ses3 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,])

# gen 2
gen2_ses3 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,])

# gen 3
gen3_ses3 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,])

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,],gen1_ses3) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,],gen1_ses3)
```

### DNAm Measures

```{r}
# associations of DNAm measures
## first gen
#horvath1
horvath1_ses3 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,])

#hannum
hannum_ses3 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,])

#horvath2
horvath2_ses3 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,])

#pedbe
pedbe_ses3 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_horvath1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,])

## second gen
#phenoage
phenoage_ses3 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,])

#grimage
grimage_ses3 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,])

## third gen
#dunedinpoam
poam_ses3 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,])

#dunedinpace
pace_ses3 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,])

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,],horvath1_ses3) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,],horvath1_ses3)
```

### Age on a granular level

```{r}
data_ses_child <- data_ses %>%
  filter(SES_time == 3)

subset_data <- data_ses_child[data_ses_child$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1, ]


#so DunedinPoAm, only late childhood and adolescence
table(subset_data$Age_DNAm_prenatal)
table(subset_data$Age_DNAm_earlychild)
table(subset_data$Age_DNAm_latechild) #6 ES for late childhood
table(subset_data$Age_DNAm_adol)      #17 ES for adolescence

#Is the association between Childhood SES and Childhood DunedinPoAm different across more granular level of age bins?

#Age_DNAm_earlychild ref cat
#No Significant difference between the two age bins
poam_latechild <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Age_DNAm_adol, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), 
       data=data_ses_child[data_ses_child$DNAm_dunedinpoam ==1, ]) 

poam_adolescent <-rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Age_DNAm_latechild, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), 
       data=data_ses_child[data_ses_child$DNAm_dunedinpoam ==1, ]) 

#also not when looking at it continiously
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Age_DNAm_Mean, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), 
       data=data_ses_child[data_ses_child$DNAm_dunedinpoam ==1, ]) 

#see percentages of how variance is distributed 
variance_distribution(data_ses_child[data_ses_child$DNAm_dunedinpoam ==1,],poam_latechild)

#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses_child[data_ses_child$DNAm_dunedinpoam ==1,],poam_latechild)






```

### Moderators: Saliva vs Blood
For exploratory analyses, test if effect sizes for childhood SES and childhood DNAm differ when samples come from saliva or venous blood. Only Horvath1, Hannum and DunedinPoAm have enough effect sizes (i.e. at least 5) for analyses.

```{r}
data_ses_child <- data_ses %>%
  filter(SES_time == 3)

#Horvath1
#saliva as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_cord + Tissue_blood_ven + Tissue_buccal, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses_child[data_ses_child$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])
#blood as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_cord + Tissue_saliva + Tissue_buccal, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses_child[data_ses_child$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

#Hannum
#saliva as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_cord + Tissue_blood_ven, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses_child[data_ses_child$DNAm_hannum == 1,])
#blood as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_cord + Tissue_saliva, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses_child[data_ses_child$DNAm_hannum == 1,])

#DunedinPoAm
#saliva as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_blood_ven, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses_child[data_ses_child$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])
#blood as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Tissue_saliva, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses_child[data_ses_child$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])
```


### Figure

```{r}
#get estimates, lower and higher cis for graphs (childhood ses-childhood dnam dataset)
ests <- c(coef(gen1_ses3)[1], coef(horvath1_ses3)[1], coef(hannum_ses3)[1], coef(horvath2_ses3)[1],
          coef(pedbe_ses3)[1], coef(gen2_ses3)[1], coef(phenoage_ses3)[1],
          coef(grimage_ses3)[1], coef(gen3_ses3)[1], coef(poam_ses3)[1], coef(pace_ses3)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1_ses3$ci.lb[1], horvath1_ses3$ci.lb[1], hannum_ses3$ci.lb[1], horvath2_ses3$ci.lb[1], 
            pedbe_ses3$ci.lb[1], gen2_ses3$ci.lb[1], phenoage_ses3$ci.lb[1], 
            grimage_ses3$ci.lb[1], gen3_ses3$ci.lb[1], poam_ses3$ci.lb[1], pace_ses3$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1_ses3$ci.ub[1], horvath1_ses3$ci.ub[1], hannum_ses3$ci.ub[1], horvath2_ses3$ci.ub[1], 
             pedbe_ses3$ci.ub[1], gen2_ses3$ci.ub[1], phenoage_ses3$ci.ub[1], 
             grimage_ses3$ci.ub[1], gen3_ses3$ci.ub[1], poam_ses3$ci.ub[1], pace_ses3$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1_ses3$pval[1], horvath1_ses3$pval[1], hannum_ses3$pval[1], horvath2_ses3$pval[1], pedbe_ses3$pval[1], 
           gen2_ses3$pval[1], phenoage_ses3$pval[1], grimage_ses3$pval[1], gen3_ses3$pval[1], 
           poam_ses3$pval[1], pace_ses3$pval[1])
  
es_no <- c(sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,]$DNAm_gen1), sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,]$DNAm_horvath1), 
           sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,]$DNAm_hannum),sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,]$DNAm_horvath2),
          sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,]$DNAm_pedbe),sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,]$DNAm_gen2), 
          sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,]$DNAm_phenoage),sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,]$DNAm_grimage),
          sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,]$DNAm_gen3),sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,]$DNAm_dunedinpoam),
          sum(data_ses[data_ses$SES_time3 == 1,]$DNAm_dunedinpace))


# convert all z's to r
ests <- convert_z2r(ests)
lowcis <- convert_z2r(lowcis)
highcis <- convert_z2r(highcis)


# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Hannum", "Horvath Skin & Blood", "PedBE",
                   "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "3rd Generation", "DunedinPoAm", "DunedinPACE"),           # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no,                        # number of effect sizes
  p_val = round(pvals,2))

png("child_ses_child_dnam.png", width = 38, height = 25, units = "cm", res = 600)

par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))

# Create the forest plot for the summary effects

forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Pearson's r [95% CI])", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = cbind(summary_effects$ES_no, summary_effects$p_val), ilab.xpos = cbind(-0.3, 0.47),
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 2,
       cex.axis = 1.8,
       textpos = as.numeric(c(-0.6, 0.37)),
       xlim = c(-0.6,0.5),
       alim = c(-0.25,0.15))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits



# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 2.5, par("usr")[2], 3.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 3
rect(par("usr")[1], 5.5, par("usr")[2], 6.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 6
rect(par("usr")[1], 10.5, par("usr")[2], 11.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 12

text(-0.29, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

text(0.45, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "p-value", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

title("Association of Childhood SES and DNAm Measures\nof Biological Aging Measured During Childhood", line = 0, cex.main = 2.5)

# Add * significant results
for (n in 1:length(pvals)) {
  if (pvals[n] < 0.05) {
    mtext("*", side = 4, line = 1, cex = 3, las = 1, at = (length(pvals)-n+1))
  }
}

dev.off()
```

## Child and adult DNAm with SES

This part examines if epigenetic clock associations with SES significantly differed by measurement timing.

```{r}

# Function to get SE from 95% CI
se_from_ci <- function(ci_lb, ci_ub) {
  (ci_ub - ci_lb) / (2 * 1.96)
}

# Input data DunedinPoAm
# Estimates from Figure 4 BCD
r <- c(-0.13, -0.07, -0.17)
ci_lb <- c(-0.15, -0.11, -0.24)
ci_ub <- c(-0.10, -0.04, -0.11)
sei <- se_from_ci(ci_lb, ci_ub)
group <- factor(c("Adults", "ChildAdults", "Children")) 
#child adults = ses childhood-dnamadults
#adults = ses adults dnam adults
#children = ses children dnam children

#set children as reference category
# Set factor levels and make "Children" the reference category
group <- factor(c("Adults", "ChildAdults", "Children"), 
                levels = c("Children", "ChildAdults", "Adults"))
dat <- data.frame(r, sei, group)

# Run fixed-effects model with group as moderator
res <- rma(yi = r, sei = sei, mods = ~ group, method = "FE", data = dat)
summary(res)

# Set factor levels and make "ChildAdults" the reference category
group <- factor(c("Adults", "ChildAdults", "Children"), 
                levels = c("ChildAdults", "Adults", "Children"))
dat <- data.frame(r, sei, group)
res <- rma(yi = r, sei = sei, mods = ~ group, method = "FE", data = dat)
summary(res)

# Input data GrimAge
# Estimates from Figure 4 BCD
r <- c(-0.15, -0.10, -0.07)
ci_lb <- c(-0.17, -0.13, -0.17)
ci_ub <- c(-0.13, -0.07, 0.03)
sei <- se_from_ci(ci_lb, ci_ub)
group <- factor(c("Adults", "ChildAdults", "Children")) 
#child adults = ses childhood-dnamadults
#adults = ses adults dnam adults
#children = ses children dnam children
dat <- data.frame(r, sei, group)

#set children as reference category
# Set factor levels and make "Children" the reference category
group <- factor(c("Adults", "ChildAdults", "Children"), 
                levels = c("Children", "Adults", "ChildAdults"))
dat <- data.frame(r, sei, group)

# Run fixed-effects model with group as moderator
res <- rma(yi = r, sei = sei, mods = ~ group, method = "FE", data = dat)
summary(res)

# Set factor levels and make "ChildAdults" the reference category
group <- factor(c("Adults", "ChildAdults", "Children"), 
                levels = c("ChildAdults", "Adults", "Children"))
dat <- data.frame(r, sei, group)
res <- rma(yi = r, sei = sei, mods = ~ group, method = "FE", data = dat)
summary(res)

```

# Race

This part runs analyses for (1) Black vs White and (2) Latinx vs White. Same structure as above - first generation analysis, then DNAm, then create a forest plot, then run moderator analyses for individual DNAm measures (where available).

## Black - White

```{r}
# create data frame for Black vs White (all from the USA, so no country filter is added)
black <- data_race %>%
  filter(Race_category == "Black") 

```

### Generations

```{r}
# associations per generation
# gen 1
gen1_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

robust(gen1_black, cluster = black$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

# gen 2
gen2_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

robust(gen2_black, cluster = black$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

# gen 3
gen3_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

robust(gen3_black, cluster = black$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation analyses (since the meta-analytic model is the same for all generations, running this once should be enough)
variance_distribution(black,gen1_black) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(black,gen1_black)
```

### DNAm Measures

```{r}
# associations of DNAm measures
## first gen
#horvath1
horvath1_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

robust(horvath1_black, cluster = black$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

#hannum
hannum_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

robust(hannum_black, cluster = black$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

#horvath2
horvath2_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

robust(horvath2_black, cluster = black$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

#zhang
zhang_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_horvath1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

robust(zhang_black, cluster = black$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

## second gen
#phenoage
phenoage_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

robust(phenoage_black, cluster = black$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

#grimage
grimage_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

robust(grimage_black, cluster = black$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

## third gen
#dunedinpoam
poam_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

robust(poam_black, cluster = black$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

#dunedinpace
pace_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

robust(pace_black, cluster = black$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for DNAm-level analyses (since the meta-analytic model is the same for all DNAm levels, running this once should be enough)
variance_distribution(black,horvath1_black) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(black,horvath1_black)
```

### Figure

```{r}
#get estimates, lower and higher cis for graphs (white vs black)
ests <- c(coef(gen1_black)[1], coef(horvath1_black)[1], coef(hannum_black)[1], coef(horvath2_black)[1], 
          coef(zhang_black)[1], coef(gen2_black)[1], coef(phenoage_black)[1], coef(grimage_black)[1], 
          coef(gen3_black)[1], coef(poam_black)[1], coef(pace_black)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1_black$ci.lb[1], horvath1_black$ci.lb[1], hannum_black$ci.lb[1], horvath2_black$ci.lb[1],
```
