```{r}
            zhang_black$ci.lb[1], gen2_black$ci.lb[1], phenoage_black$ci.lb[1], grimage_black$ci.lb[1], 
            gen3_black$ci.lb[1], poam_black$ci.lb[1], pace_black$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1_black$ci.ub[1], horvath1_black$ci.ub[1], hannum_black$ci.ub[1], horvath2_black$ci.ub[1], 
            zhang_black$ci.ub[1], gen2_black$ci.ub[1], phenoage_black$ci.ub[1], grimage_black$ci.ub[1], 
            gen3_black$ci.ub[1], poam_black$ci.ub[1], pace_black$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1_black$pval[1], horvath1_black$pval[1], hannum_black$pval[1], horvath2_black$pval[1], 
           zhang_black$pval[1], gen2_black$pval[1], phenoage_black$pval[1], grimage_black$pval[1], 
           gen3_black$pval[1], poam_black$pval[1], pace_black$pval[1])
  
es_no <- c(sum(black$DNAm_gen1), sum(black$DNAm_horvath1), sum(black$DNAm_hannum), sum(black$DNAm_horvath2),
          sum(black$DNAm_zhang), sum(black$DNAm_gen2), sum(black$DNAm_phenoage), sum(black$DNAm_grimage),
           sum(black$DNAm_gen3), sum(black$DNAm_dunedinpoam), sum(black$DNAm_dunedinpace))


# convert all z's to r
ests <- convert_z2r(ests)
lowcis <- convert_z2r(lowcis)
highcis <- convert_z2r(highcis)

# convert all r's to d
ests <- r_to_d(ests)
lowcis <- r_to_d(lowcis)
highcis <- r_to_d(highcis)

png("black.png", width = 38, height = 25, units = "cm", res = 600)

par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))

# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Hannum", "Horvath Skin & Blood", "Zhang",
                   "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "3rd Generation", "DunedinPoAm", "DunedinPACE"),           # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no,                        # number of effect sizes
  p_val = round(pvals,2))

# Create the forest plot for the summary effects

forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Cohen's d [95% CI])", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = cbind(summary_effects$ES_no, summary_effects$p_val), ilab.xpos = cbind(-1.22, 1.75),
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 2,
       cex.axis = 1.8,
       textpos = as.numeric(c(-2.3, 1.5)),
       xlim = c(-2.3,1.9),
       alim = c(-1.1,0.7))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits


# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 2.5, par("usr")[2], 3.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.4, 0.7, 1, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 3
rect(par("usr")[1], 5.5, par("usr")[2], 6.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.4, 0.7, 1, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 6
rect(par("usr")[1], 10.5, par("usr")[2], 11.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.4, 0.7, 1, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 12

text(-1.1, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2.5)  # Adjust position of the label

text(1.72, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "p-value", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

title("Black- Compared to White-Identifying Groups", line = 0, cex.main = 2)

# Add * significant results
for (n in 1:length(pvals)) {
  if (pvals[n] < 0.05) {
    mtext("*", side = 4, line = 1, cex = 3, las = 1, at = (length(pvals)-n+1))
  }
}
dev.off()
```

### Moderators

Only ran the moderator x DNAm measure combinations with at least 5 effect sizes in more than 1 category. There were only enough to run moderators for array in Horvath1, and cell composition in Horvath1, Hannum and GrimAge. None are significant.

```{r}
# ARRAY - HORVATH1
#450k for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_epic + Array_epic_450k, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black[black$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

#EPIC for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Array_450k + Array_epic_450k, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black[black$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

# CELL COMP - HORVATH1
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black[black$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black[black$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])


# CELL COMP - HANNUM
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black[black$DNAm_hannum == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black[black$DNAm_hannum == 1,])


# CELL COMP - GRIMAGE
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black[black$DNAm_grimage == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black[black$DNAm_grimage == 1,])


```

## Latinx - White

Not enough effect sizes for moderators, so only main analyses are run - first generation level, then dnam level, and then plot.

```{r}
# create data frame for Latinx vs White (all from the USA, so no country filter is added)
latinx <- data_race %>%
  filter(Race_category == "Latinx") 
```

### Generations

```{r}
# associations per generation
# gen 1
gen1_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)

robust(gen1_latinx, cluster = latinx$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

# gen 2
gen2_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)

robust(gen2_latinx, cluster = latinx$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

# gen 3
gen3_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)

robust(gen3_latinx, cluster = latinx$Cohort_ID) #run cluster robust variance tests controlling for cohort id

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation analyses (since the meta-analytic model is the same for all generations, running this once should be enough)
variance_distribution(latinx,gen1_latinx) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(latinx,gen1_latinx)
```

### DNAm Measures

```{r}
# associations of DNAm measures
## first gen
#horvath1
horvath1_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)


#hannum
hannum_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)

#horvath2
horvath2_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)

## second gen
#phenoage
phenoage_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam , random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)

#grimage
grimage_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)

## third gen
#dunedinpoam
poam_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_horvath1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)

#dunedinpace
pace_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for DNAm analyses (since the meta-analytic model is the same for all DNAm measures, running this once should be enough)
variance_distribution(latinx,horvath1_latinx) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(latinx,horvath1_latinx)
```

### Figure

```{r}
#get estimates, lower and higher cis for graphs (white vs latinx)
ests <- c(coef(gen1_latinx)[1], coef(horvath1_latinx)[1], coef(hannum_latinx)[1], coef(horvath2_latinx)[1], 
          coef(gen2_latinx)[1], coef(phenoage_latinx)[1], coef(grimage_latinx)[1], coef(gen3_latinx)[1], 
          coef(poam_latinx)[1], coef(pace_latinx)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1_latinx$ci.lb[1], horvath1_latinx$ci.lb[1], hannum_latinx$ci.lb[1], horvath2_latinx$ci.lb[1], 
            gen2_latinx$ci.lb[1], phenoage_latinx$ci.lb[1], grimage_latinx$ci.lb[1], gen3_latinx$ci.lb[1], 
            poam_latinx$ci.lb[1], pace_latinx$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1_latinx$ci.ub[1], horvath1_latinx$ci.ub[1], hannum_latinx$ci.ub[1], horvath2_latinx$ci.ub[1], 
            gen2_latinx$ci.ub[1], phenoage_latinx$ci.ub[1], grimage_latinx$ci.ub[1], gen3_latinx$ci.ub[1], 
            poam_latinx$ci.ub[1], pace_latinx$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1_latinx$pval[1], horvath1_latinx$pval[1], hannum_latinx$pval[1], horvath2_latinx$pval[1], 
            gen2_latinx$pval[1], phenoage_latinx$pval[1], grimage_latinx$pval[1], gen3_latinx$pval[1], 
           poam_latinx$pval[1], pace_latinx$pval[1])
  
es_no <- c(sum(latinx$DNAm_gen1), sum(latinx$DNAm_horvath1), sum(latinx$DNAm_hannum), sum(latinx$DNAm_horvath2),
          sum(latinx$DNAm_gen2), sum(latinx$DNAm_phenoage), sum(latinx$DNAm_grimage),
           sum(latinx$DNAm_gen3), sum(latinx$DNAm_dunedinpoam), sum(latinx$DNAm_dunedinpace))


# convert all z's to r
ests <- convert_z2r(ests)
lowcis <- convert_z2r(lowcis)
highcis <- convert_z2r(highcis)

# convert all r's to d
ests <- r_to_d(ests)
lowcis <- r_to_d(lowcis)
highcis <- r_to_d(highcis)


# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Hannum", "Horvath Skin & Blood",
                   "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "3rd Generation", "DunedinPoAm", "DunedinPACE"),           # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no,                        # number of effect sizes
  p_val = round(pvals,2))

png("latinx.png", width = 38, height = 23, units = "cm", res = 600)
par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))

# Create the forest plot for the summary effects

forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Cohen's d [95% CI])", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = cbind(summary_effects$ES_no, summary_effects$p_val), ilab.xpos = cbind(-1.22, 1.75),
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 2,
       cex.axis = 1.8,
       textpos = as.numeric(c(-2.3, 1.5)),
       xlim = c(-2.3,1.9),
       alim = c(-1.1,0.7))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits


# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 2.5, par("usr")[2], 3.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.4, 0.7, 1, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 3
rect(par("usr")[1], 5.5, par("usr")[2], 6.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.4, 0.7, 1, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 6
rect(par("usr")[1], 9.5, par("usr")[2], 10.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.4, 0.7, 1, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 12

text(-1.1, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2.5)  # Adjust position of the label

text(1.72, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "p-value", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

title("Latinx- Compared to White-Identifying Groups", line = 0, cex.main = 2)

# Add * significant results
for (n in 1:length(pvals)) {
  if (pvals[n] < 0.05) {
    mtext("*", side = 4, line = 1, cex = 3, las = 1, at = (length(pvals)-n+1))
  }
}

dev.off()
```

# Change Analysis

For change analysis, first calculate SE from CIs if needed, and reverse code the effect size (unstandardized b) to put all effect sizes in the same direction.

(removed 5 effect sizes, 3 bc it measured change in dnam vs race, and 2 bc they only had pearson's r reported, not b)

```{r change}

# for change data, calculate SE from CIs when not available
# (upper limit - lower limit)/(2*1.96)
# and reverse code b when necessary

for (c in 1:nrow(change_data)) {
  if (is.na(change_data$Effect_Size_SE[c])) {
    change_data$Effect_Size_SE[c] <- (as.numeric(change_data$Effect_Size_higherCI[c]) - as.numeric(change_data$Effect_Size_lowerCI[c]))/((2*1.96))
  }
  
  if (is.na(change_data$Reverse_coding[c]) == FALSE && change_data$Reverse_coding[c] == 1) {
    change_data$unstandardized_b[c] <- as.numeric(change_data$unstandardized_b[c]) * -1
  }
}

```

## Change in DNAm only

Analyze generation differences for effect sizes reporting on SES x change in DNAm. Generation-level analysis, DNAm measure-level analysis and plot are all in one chunk.

```{r change-dnam}
# separate data frame and run analysis
change_dnam <- change_data %>%
  filter(change_measure == 1)

# run analysis by generation
## generation 1 as reference
gen1_dnamchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam)

## generation 2 as reference
gen2_dnamchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam)

## generation 3 as reference
gen3_dnamchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam)


# run analysis by dnam measure
## horvath1
horvath1_dnamchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_grimage + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam)

## horvath2
horvath2_dnamchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_grimage + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam)

## phenoage
phenoage_dnamchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_grimage + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam)

## grimage
grimage_dnamchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam)

## dunedinpoam
poam_dnamchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam)


# plot
# Extract the coefficients 
ests <- c(coef(gen1_dnamchange)[1], coef(horvath1_dnamchange)[1], coef(horvath2_dnamchange)[1], coef(gen2_dnamchange)[1], coef(phenoage_dnamchange)[1], coef(grimage_dnamchange)[1], coef(gen3_dnamchange)[1], coef(poam_dnamchange)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1_dnamchange$ci.lb[1], horvath1_dnamchange$ci.lb[1], horvath2_dnamchange$ci.lb[1], gen2_dnamchange$ci.lb[1], phenoage_dnamchange$ci.lb[1], grimage_dnamchange$ci.lb[1], gen3_dnamchange$ci.lb[1], poam_dnamchange$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1_dnamchange$ci.ub[1], horvath1_dnamchange$ci.ub[1], horvath2_dnamchange$ci.ub[1], gen2_dnamchange$ci.ub[1], phenoage_dnamchange$ci.ub[1], grimage_dnamchange$ci.ub[1], gen3_dnamchange$ci.ub[1], poam_dnamchange$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1_dnamchange$pval[1], horvath1_dnamchange$pval[1], horvath2_dnamchange$pval[1], gen2_dnamchange$pval[1], phenoage_dnamchange$pval[1], grimage_dnamchange$pval[1], gen3_dnamchange$pval[1], poam_dnamchange$pval[1])

es_no <- c(sum(change_dnam$DNAm_gen1), sum(change_dnam$DNAm_horvath1), sum(change_dnam$DNAm_horvath2), sum(change_dnam$DNAm_gen2), sum(change_dnam$DNAm_phenoage), sum(change_dnam$DNAm_grimage),sum(change_dnam$DNAm_gen3), sum(change_dnam$DNAm_dunedinpoam))


png("change_dnam.png", width = 38, height = 23, units = "cm", res = 600)

par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))

# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Horvath Skin & Blood", "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "3rd Generation", "DunedinPoAm"),      # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no,                        # number of effect sizes
  p_val = round(pvals,2))

# Create the forest plot for the summary effects

forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Unstandardized b [95% CI])", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = cbind(summary_effects$ES_no, summary_effects$p_val), ilab.xpos = cbind(-1.4,2.2),
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 2,
       cex.axis = 1.8,
       textpos = as.numeric(c(-2.7, 1.9)),
       xlim = c(-2.7,2.4),
       alim = c(-1.2,1))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits



# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 1.5, par("usr")[2], 2.5, border = NA, col= rgb(1, 0.8, 0, alpha=0.4))  # Shading for row 2
rect(par("usr")[1], 4.5, par("usr")[2], 5.5, border = NA, col= rgb(1, 0.8, 0, alpha=0.4))  # Shading for row 5
rect(par("usr")[1], 7.5, par("usr")[2], 8.5, border = NA, col= rgb(1, 0.8, 0, alpha=0.4))  # Shading for row 8

text(-1.25, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.05, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

text(2.18, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "p-value", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

title("Association of SES and Change in DNAm Measures of Biological Aging", line = 0, cex.main = 2.5)

dev.off()
```

## Change in SES only

Analyze generation differences for effect sizes reporting on change in SES x DNAm. All effect sizes report intergenerational social class/SES change (i.e. difference between parental and own status). Generation-level analysis, DNAm measure-level analysis and plot are all in one chunk.

```{r change-ses}

change_ses <- change_data %>%
  filter(change_measure == 2)

# run analysis by generation
## generation 1 as reference
gen1_seschange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_ses)

## generation 2 as reference
gen2_seschange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_ses)


# run analysis by dnam measure
## horvath1
horvath1_seschange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_grimage, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_ses)

## hannum
hannum_seschange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_grimage, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_ses)

## phenoage
phenoage_seschange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_grimage, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_ses)

## grimage
grimage_seschange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_horvath1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_ses)


# Extract the coefficients 
ests <- c(coef(gen1_seschange)[1], coef(horvath1_seschange)[1], coef(hannum_seschange)[1], coef(gen2_seschange)[1], coef(phenoage_seschange)[1], coef(grimage_seschange)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1_seschange$ci.lb[1], horvath1_seschange$ci.lb[1], hannum_seschange$ci.lb[1], gen2_seschange$ci.lb[1], phenoage_seschange$ci.lb[1], grimage_seschange$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1_seschange$ci.ub[1], horvath1_seschange$ci.ub[1], hannum_seschange$ci.ub[1], gen2_seschange$ci.ub[1], phenoage_seschange$ci.ub[1], grimage_seschange$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1_seschange$pval[1], horvath1_seschange$pval[1], hannum_seschange$pval[1], gen2_seschange$pval[1], phenoage_seschange$pval[1], grimage_seschange$pval[1])
  
es_no <- c(sum(change_ses$DNAm_gen1), sum(change_ses$DNAm_horvath1), sum(change_ses$DNAm_hannum), sum(change_ses$DNAm_gen2), sum(change_ses$DNAm_phenoage), sum(change_ses$DNAm_grimage))


# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Hannum", "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge"),      # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no,                        # number of effect sizes
  p_val = round(pvals,2))

png("change_ses.png", width = 38, height = 20, units = "cm", res = 600)

par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))

# Create the forest plot for the summary effects
forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Unstandardized b [95% CI])", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = cbind(summary_effects$ES_no, summary_effects$p_val), ilab.xpos = cbind(-3.7,2.05),
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 1.8,
       cex.axis = 2,
       textpos = as.numeric(c(-5.55, 1.65)),
       xlim = c(-5.55,2.25),
       alim = c(-3.5,0.2))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits


# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 2.5, par("usr")[2], 3.5, border = NA, col= rgb(1, 0.8, 0, alpha=0.4))  # Shading for row 3
rect(par("usr")[1], 5.5, par("usr")[2], 6.5, border = NA, col= rgb(1, 0.8, 0, alpha=0.4))  # Shading for row 6

text(-3.5, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.05, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

text(1.97, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "p-value", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

# Add * significant results
for (n in 1:length(pvals)) {
  if (pvals[n] < 0.05) {
    mtext("*", side = 4, line = 1, cex = 3, las = 1, at = (length(pvals)-n+1))
  }
}


title("Association of Intergenerational SES Change and DNAm Measures\nof Biological Aging", line = 0, cex.main = 2.5)

dev.off()
```

## Change in DNAm and SES

Analyze generation differences for effect sizes reporting on change in SES x change in DNAm. Generation-level analysis, DNAm measure-level analysis and plot are all in one chunk.

```{r change-dnam-ses}
# separate data frame and run analysis
## change in dnam and ses
change_dnam_ses <- change_data %>%
  filter(change_measure == 3)

# run analysis by generation
## generation 1 as reference
gen1_bothchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam_ses)

## generation 2 as reference
gen2_bothchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam_ses)

## generation 3 as reference
gen3_bothchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam_ses)


# run analysis by dnam measure
## horvath1
horvath1_bothchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_pedbe + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_grimage + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_dunedinpace, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam_ses)

## hannum
hannum_bothchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_pedbe + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_grimage + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_dunedinpace, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam_ses)

## horvath2
horvath2_bothchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_pedbe + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_grimage + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_dunedinpace, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam_ses)

## pedbe
pedbe_bothchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_grimage + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_dunedinpace, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam_ses)

## phenoage
phenoage_bothchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_pedbe + DNAm_grimage + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_dunedinpace, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam_ses)

## grimage
grimage_bothchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_pedbe + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_dunedinpace, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam_ses)

## dunedinpoam
poam_bothchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_pedbe + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_grimage + DNAm_dunedinpace, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam_ses)

## dunedinpace
pace_bothchange <- rma.mv(unstandardized_b, Effect_Size_SE, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_pedbe + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_grimage + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=change_dnam_ses)


# plot
# Extract the coefficients 
ests <- c(coef(gen1_bothchange)[1], coef(horvath1_bothchange)[1], coef(hannum_bothchange)[1], coef(horvath2_bothchange)[1], coef(pedbe_bothchange)[1], coef(gen2_bothchange)[1], coef(phenoage_bothchange)[1], coef(grimage_bothchange)[1], coef(gen3_bothchange)[1], coef(poam_bothchange)[1], coef(pace_bothchange)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1_bothchange$ci.lb[1], horvath1_bothchange$ci.lb[1], hannum_bothchange$ci.lb[1], horvath2_bothchange$ci.lb[1], pedbe_bothchange$ci.lb[1], gen2_bothchange$ci.lb[1], phenoage_bothchange$ci.lb[1], grimage_bothchange$ci.lb[1], gen3_bothchange$ci.lb[1], poam_bothchange$ci.lb[1], pace_bothchange$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1_bothchange$ci.ub[1], horvath1_bothchange$ci.ub[1], hannum_bothchange$ci.ub[1], horvath2_bothchange$ci.ub[1], pedbe_bothchange$ci.ub[1], gen2_bothchange$ci.ub[1], phenoage_bothchange$ci.ub[1], grimage_bothchange$ci.ub[1], gen3_bothchange$ci.ub[1], poam_bothchange$ci.ub[1], pace_bothchange$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1_bothchange$pval[1], horvath1_bothchange$pval[1], hannum_bothchange$pval[1], horvath2_bothchange$pval[1], pedbe_bothchange$pval[1], gen2_bothchange$pval[1], phenoage_bothchange$pval[1], grimage_bothchange$pval[1], gen3_bothchange$pval[1], poam_bothchange$pval[1], pace_bothchange$pval[1])
  
es_no <- c(sum(change_dnam_ses$DNAm_gen1), sum(change_dnam_ses$DNAm_horvath1), sum(change_dnam_ses$DNAm_hannum),sum(change_dnam_ses$DNAm_horvath2), sum(change_dnam_ses$DNAm_pedbe), sum(change_dnam_ses$DNAm_gen2), sum(change_dnam_ses$DNAm_phenoage), sum(change_dnam_ses$DNAm_grimage),sum(change_dnam_ses$DNAm_gen3), sum(change_dnam_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam),sum(change_dnam_ses$DNAm_dunedinpace))


# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Hannum", "Horvath Skin & Blood", "PedBE", "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "3rd Generation", "DunedinPoAm", "DunedinPACE"),      # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no,                        # number of effect sizes
  p_val = round(pvals,2))

png("change_ses_dnam.png", width = 38, height = 25, units = "cm", res = 600)

par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))

# Create the forest plot for the summary effects
forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Unstandardized b [95% CI])", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = cbind(summary_effects$ES_no, summary_effects$p_val), ilab.xpos = cbind(-5,6.6),
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 1.8,
       cex.axis = 2,
       textpos = as.numeric(c(-9, 5.5)),
       xlim = c(-9,7),
       alim = c(-4.5,2.5))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits


# Add * significant results
for (n in 1:length(pvals)) {
  if (pvals[n] < 0.05) {
    mtext("*", side = 4, line = 1, cex = 3, las = 1, at = (length(pvals)-n+1))
  }
}

# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 2.5, par("usr")[2], 3.5, border = NA, col= rgb(1, 0.8, 0, alpha=0.4))  # Shading for row 3
rect(par("usr")[1], 5.5, par("usr")[2], 6.5, border = NA, col= rgb(1, 0.8, 0, alpha=0.4))  # Shading for row 6
rect(par("usr")[1], 10.5, par("usr")[2], 11.5, border = NA, col= rgb(1, 0.8, 0, alpha=0.4))  # Shading for row 11

text(-4.8, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

text(6.4, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "p-value", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

title("Association of Change in SES and Change in DNAm Measures\nof Biological Aging", line = 0, cex.main = 2.5)

dev.off()
```

# Bicor Analysis

Analyze the bicor data (which we couldnt transform to Pearson's r), in one chunk: calculate standard errors, run generation-level and dnam-level analysis, and plot. There are effect sizes only from first and second generation measures. When running the analyses, there's a warning that there's no variance at the cohort level, because all effect sizes come from the Women's Health Initiative.

```{r bicor}

# calculate standard errors of bicor estimates using the steps given by chatgpt and formula from wilson's practical meta-analysis calculator

for (b in 1:nrow(bicor_data)) {
  df <- bicor_data$Effect_Size_n[b] - 2
  p <- as.numeric(bicor_data$p.value[b])
  t <- abs(qt(p/2,df))
  se <- bicor_data$bicor[b]/t
  v <- se^2
  if (v == 0) {
    v <- 0.0000001
  }
  bicor_data$bicor_var[b] <- v
}

# run meta-analysis: we get a warning because theres no variance at cohort level, since all effect sizes come from WHI
# run analysis by generation
## generation 1 as reference
gen1_bicor <- rma.mv(bicor, bicor_var, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=bicor_data)

## generation 2 as reference
gen2_bicor <- rma.mv(bicor, bicor_var, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=bicor_data)

# run analysis by dnam measure
## horvath1
horvath1_bicor <- rma.mv(bicor, bicor_var, mods = ~ DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_grimage2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=bicor_data)

## hannum
hannum_bicor <- rma.mv(bicor, bicor_var, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_grimage2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=bicor_data)

## horvath2
horvath2_bicor <- rma.mv(bicor, bicor_var, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_grimage + DNAm_grimage2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=bicor_data)

## phenoage
phenoage_bicor <- rma.mv(bicor, bicor_var, mods = ~ DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_grimage2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=bicor_data)

## grimage
grimage_bicor <- rma.mv(bicor, bicor_var, mods = ~ DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_grimage2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=bicor_data)

## grimage2
grimage2_bicor <- rma.mv(bicor, bicor_var, mods = ~ DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_grimage, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=bicor_data)



# PLOT

# Extract the coefficients 

ests <- c(coef(gen1_bicor)[1], coef(horvath1_bicor)[1], coef(hannum_bicor)[1], coef(horvath2_bicor)[1],coef(gen2_bicor)[1], coef(phenoage_bicor)[1], coef(grimage_bicor)[1], coef(grimage2_bicor)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1_bicor$ci.lb[1], horvath1_bicor$ci.lb[1], hannum_bicor$ci.lb[1], horvath2_bicor$ci.lb[1], gen2_bicor$ci.lb[1], phenoage_bicor$ci.lb[1], grimage_bicor$ci.lb[1], grimage2_bicor$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1_bicor$ci.ub[1], horvath1_bicor$ci.ub[1], hannum_bicor$ci.ub[1], horvath2_bicor$ci.ub[1], gen2_bicor$ci.ub[1], phenoage_bicor$ci.ub[1], grimage_bicor$ci.ub[1], grimage2_bicor$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1_bicor$pval[1], horvath1_bicor$pval[1], hannum_bicor$pval[1], horvath2_bicor$pval[1], gen2_bicor$pval[1], phenoage_bicor$pval[1], grimage_bicor$pval[1], grimage2_bicor$pval[1])

es_no <- c(sum(bicor_data$DNAm_gen1), sum(bicor_data$DNAm_horvath1), sum(bicor_data$DNAm_hannum),sum(bicor_data$DNAm_horvath2), sum(bicor_data$DNAm_gen2), sum(bicor_data$DNAm_phenoage), sum(bicor_data$DNAm_grimage),sum(bicor_data$DNAm_grimage2))


# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Hannum", "Horvath Skin & Blood", "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "GrimAge2"),      # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no,                        # number of effect sizes
  p_val = round(pvals,2))

png("bicor.png", width = 38, height = 25, units = "cm", res = 600)

par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))

# Create the forest plot for the summary effects
forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Pearson's r [95% CI])", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = cbind(summary_effects$ES_no, summary_effects$p_val), ilab.xpos = cbind(-0.3, 0.47),
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 2,
       cex.axis = 1.8,
       textpos = as.numeric(c(-0.6, 0.37)),
       xlim = c(-0.6,0.5),
       alim = c(-0.25,0.15))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits


# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 3.5, par("usr")[2], 4.5, border = NA, col= rgb(1, 0.6, 0.2, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 4
rect(par("usr")[1], 7.5, par("usr")[2], 8.5, border = NA, col= rgb(1, 0.6, 0.2, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 8

text(-0.3, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

text(0.45, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "p-value", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

title("Association of SES and DNAm Measures\nof Biological Aging (Biweight Midcorrelation)", line = 0, cex.main = 2.5)

# Add * significant results
for (n in 1:length(pvals)) {
  if (pvals[n] < 0.05) {
    mtext("*", side = 4, line = 1, cex = 3, las = 1, at = (length(pvals)-n+1))
  }
}

dev.off()
```

# Publication Bias

Create funnel plots for significant publication bias - for SES, Black vs White and Latinx vs White datasets.

## SES

Individual clock level publication bias is significant for SES dataset.

```{r}
#| echo: false

par(mar=c(5,8,5,8), mgp = c(0, 2.5, 0))
#assess publication bias
# clocks
## horvath1
eggers_horvath1 <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])
reg_horvath1 <- regtest(eggers_horvath1)
taf_horvath1 <- trimfill(eggers_horvath1)

funnel(eggers_horvath1, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.6, 0, 0.6))

## hannum
eggers_hannum <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_hannum == 1,])
reg_hannum <- regtest(eggers_hannum)
taf_hannum <- trimfill(eggers_hannum)

funnel(eggers_hannum, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.6, 0, 0.6))


## horvath2
eggers_horvath2 <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath2 == 1,])
reg_horvath2 <- regtest(eggers_horvath2)
taf_horvath2 <- trimfill(eggers_horvath2)

funnel(eggers_horvath2, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.4, -0.1, 0.35))


## zhang
eggers_zhang <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_zhang == 1,])
reg_zhang <- regtest(eggers_zhang)
taf_zhang <- trimfill(eggers_zhang)

funnel(eggers_zhang, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.3, 0, 0.3))


## pedbe
eggers_pedbe <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_pedbe == 1,])
reg_pedbe <- regtest(eggers_pedbe)
taf_pedbe <- trimfill(eggers_pedbe)

funnel(eggers_pedbe, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.35, 0, 0.35))


## phenoage
eggers_phenoage <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])
reg_phenoage <- regtest(eggers_phenoage)
taf_phenoage <- trimfill(eggers_phenoage)

funnel(eggers_phenoage, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.6, -0.1, 0.5))


## grimage
eggers_grimage <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,])
reg_grimage <- regtest(eggers_grimage)
taf_grimage <- trimfill(eggers_grimage)

funnel(eggers_grimage, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.7, -0.15, 0.45))


## poam
eggers_poam <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])
reg_poam <- regtest(eggers_poam)
taf_poam <- trimfill(eggers_poam)

funnel(eggers_poam, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.4, -0.1, 0.2))


## pace
eggers_pace <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpace == 1,])
reg_pace <- regtest(eggers_pace)
taf_pace <- trimfill(eggers_pace)

funnel(eggers_pace, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.35, -0.15, 0.05))

```


## Black vs White

```{r}
#| echo: false

par(mar=c(5,8,5,8), mgp = c(0, 2.5, 0))
#assess publication bias
# clocks
## horvath1
eggers_horvath1_bl <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=black[black$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])
reg_horvath1_bl <- regtest(eggers_horvath1_bl)
taf_horvath1_bl <- trimfill(eggers_horvath1_bl)

funnel(eggers_horvath1_bl, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.6, 0, 0.6))

## hannum
eggers_hannum_bl <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=black[black$DNAm_hannum == 1,])
reg_hannum_bl <- regtest(eggers_hannum_bl)
taf_hannum_bl <- trimfill(eggers_hannum_bl)

funnel(eggers_hannum_bl, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.6, 0, 0.6))


## horvath2
eggers_horvath2_bl <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=black[black$DNAm_horvath2 == 1,])
reg_horvath2_bl <- regtest(eggers_horvath2_bl)
taf_horvath2_bl <- trimfill(eggers_horvath2_bl)

funnel(eggers_horvath2_bl, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.4, -0.1, 0.35))


## zhang
eggers_zhang_bl <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=black[black$DNAm_zhang == 1,])
reg_zhang_bl <- regtest(eggers_zhang_bl)
taf_zhang_bl <- trimfill(eggers_zhang_bl)

funnel(eggers_zhang_bl, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.3, 0, 0.3))



## phenoage
eggers_phenoage_bl <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=black[black$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])
reg_phenoage_bl <- regtest(eggers_phenoage_bl)
taf_phenoage_bl <- trimfill(eggers_phenoage_bl)

funnel(eggers_phenoage_bl, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.6, -0.1, 0.5))


## grimage
eggers_grimage_bl <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=black[black$DNAm_grimage == 1,])
reg_grimage_bl <- regtest(eggers_grimage_bl)
taf_grimage_bl <- trimfill(eggers_grimage_bl)

funnel(eggers_grimage_bl, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.7, -0.15, 0.45))


## poam
eggers_poam_bl <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=black[black$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])
reg_poam_bl <- regtest(eggers_poam_bl)
taf_poam_bl <- trimfill(eggers_poam_bl)

funnel(eggers_poam_bl, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.4, -0.1, 0.2))


## pace
eggers_pace_bl <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=black[black$DNAm_dunedinpace == 1,])
reg_pace_bl <- regtest(eggers_pace_bl)
taf_pace_bl <- trimfill(eggers_pace_bl)

funnel(eggers_pace_bl, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.35, -0.15, 0.05))

```


## Latinx vs White
```{r}
#| echo: false

par(mar=c(5,8,5,8), mgp = c(0, 2.5, 0))
#assess publication bias
# clocks
## horvath1
eggers_horvath1_la <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=latinx[latinx$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])
reg_horvath1_la <- regtest(eggers_horvath1_la)
taf_horvath1_la <- trimfill(eggers_horvath1_la)

funnel(eggers_horvath1_la, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.6, 0, 0.6))

## hannum
eggers_hannum_la <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=latinx[latinx$DNAm_hannum == 1,])
reg_hannum_la <- regtest(eggers_hannum_la)
taf_hannum_la <- trimfill(eggers_hannum_la)

funnel(eggers_hannum_la, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.6, 0, 0.6))


## horvath2
eggers_horvath2_la <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=latinx[latinx$DNAm_horvath2 == 1,])
reg_horvath2_la <- regtest(eggers_horvath2_la)
taf_horvath2_la <- trimfill(eggers_horvath2_la)

funnel(eggers_horvath2_la, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.4, -0.1, 0.35))


## phenoage
eggers_phenoage_la <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=latinx[latinx$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])
reg_phenoage_la <- regtest(eggers_phenoage_la)
taf_phenoage_la <- trimfill(eggers_phenoage_la)

funnel(eggers_phenoage_la, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.6, -0.1, 0.5))


## grimage
eggers_grimage_la <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=latinx[latinx$DNAm_grimage == 1,])
reg_grimage_la <- regtest(eggers_grimage_la)
taf_grimage_la <- trimfill(eggers_grimage_la)

funnel(eggers_grimage_la, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.7, -0.15, 0.45))


## poam
eggers_poam_la <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=latinx[latinx$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])
reg_poam_la <- regtest(eggers_poam_la)
taf_poam_la <- trimfill(eggers_poam_la)

funnel(eggers_poam_la, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.4, -0.1, 0.2))


## pace
eggers_pace_la <- rma(yi=Fisher_z,vi=z_variance, data=latinx[latinx$DNAm_dunedinpace == 1,])
reg_pace_la <- regtest(eggers_pace_la)
taf_pace_la <- trimfill(eggers_pace_la)

funnel(eggers_pace_la, cex.axis = 5, ylab = "", xlab = "", cex = 3.5, steps = 3, at = c(-0.35, -0.15, 0.05))

```


# Figures by Array

Run generation-level and DNAm-level analyses for EPIC and 450k arrays separately, then create forest plots.

## EPIC

### Analyses

```{r}
epic <- data_ses %>%
  filter(Array_epic == 1)

# associations per generation
# gen 1
gen1_epic <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=epic)
# gen 2
gen2_epic <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=epic)
# gen 3
gen3_epic <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=epic)

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(epic,gen1_epic) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(epic,gen1_epic)

# associations of DNAm measures
## first gen
#horvath1
horvath1_epic <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=epic)

#hannum
hannum_epic <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=epic)

#horvath2
horvath2_epic <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=epic)

#zhang
zhang_epic <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=epic)

#pedbe
pedbe_epic <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_horvath1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=epic)

## second gen
#phenoage
phenoage_epic <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=epic)

#grimage
grimage_epic <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=epic)

## third gen
#dunedinpoam
poam_epic <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=epic)

#dunedinpace
pace_epic <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=epic)

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(epic,horvath1_epic) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(epic,horvath1_epic)
```

### Figure

```{r}

#### plot ####

#get estimates, lower and higher cis for graphs (full dataset)
ests <- c(coef(gen1_epic)[1], coef(horvath1_epic)[1], coef(hannum_epic)[1], coef(horvath2_epic)[1], coef(zhang_epic)[1], coef(pedbe_epic)[1],coef(gen2_epic)[1], coef(phenoage_epic)[1], coef(grimage_epic)[1], coef(gen3_epic)[1], coef(poam_epic)[1], coef(pace_epic)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1_epic$ci.lb[1], horvath1_epic$ci.lb[1], hannum_epic$ci.lb[1], horvath2_epic$ci.lb[1], zhang_epic$ci.lb[1], pedbe_epic$ci.lb[1], gen2_epic$ci.lb[1], phenoage_epic$ci.lb[1], grimage_epic$ci.lb[1], gen3_epic$ci.lb[1], poam_epic$ci.lb[1], pace_epic$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1_epic$ci.ub[1], horvath1_epic$ci.ub[1], hannum_epic$ci.ub[1], horvath2_epic$ci.ub[1], zhang_epic$ci.ub[1], pedbe_epic$ci.ub[1],gen2_epic$ci.ub[1], phenoage_epic$ci.ub[1], grimage_epic$ci.ub[1], gen3_epic$ci.ub[1], poam_epic$ci.ub[1], pace_epic$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1_epic$pval[1], horvath1_epic$pval[1], hannum_epic$pval[1], horvath2_epic$pval[1], zhang_epic$pval[1], pedbe_epic$pval[1],gen2_epic$pval[1], phenoage_epic$pval[1], grimage_epic$pval[1], gen3_epic$pval[1], poam_epic$pval[1], pace_epic$pval[1])
  
es_no <- c(sum(epic$DNAm_gen1), sum(epic$DNAm_horvath1), sum(epic$DNAm_hannum), 
           sum(epic$DNAm_horvath2), sum(epic$DNAm_zhang), sum(epic$DNAm_pedbe), 
           sum(epic$DNAm_gen2), sum(epic$DNAm_phenoage), sum(epic$DNAm_grimage),
           sum(epic$DNAm_gen3), sum(epic$DNAm_dunedinpoam), sum(epic$DNAm_dunedinpace))


# convert all z's to r
ests <- convert_z2r(ests)
lowcis <- convert_z2r(lowcis)
highcis <- convert_z2r(highcis)


# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Hannum", "Horvath Skin & Blood", "Zhang", "PedBE",
                   "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "3rd Generation", "DunedinPoAm", "DunedinPACE"),           # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no,                        # number of effect sizes
  p_val = round(pvals,2))

png("ses_epic.png", width = 38, height = 25, units = "cm", res = 600)

par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))


# Create the forest plot for the summary effects

forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Pearson's r [95% CI])", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = cbind(summary_effects$ES_no, summary_effects$p_val), ilab.xpos = cbind(-0.39, 0.47),
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 2,
       cex.axis = 1.8,
       textpos = as.numeric(c(-0.7, 0.37)),
       xlim = c(-0.7,0.5),
       alim = c(-0.3,0.1))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits



# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 2.5, par("usr")[2], 3.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.8, 0.6, 1, alpha=0.4))  # Shading for row 3
rect(par("usr")[1], 5.5, par("usr")[2], 6.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.8, 0.6, 1, alpha=0.4))  # Shading for row 6
rect(par("usr")[1], 11.5, par("usr")[2], 12.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.8, 0.6, 1, alpha=0.4))  # Shading for row 12

text(-0.35, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

text(0.45, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "p-value", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

title("Association of Socioeconomic Status and DNAm Measures\nof Biological Aging (EPIC Array)", line = 0, cex.main = 2.5)

# Add * significant results
for (n in 1:length(pvals)) {
  if (pvals[n] < 0.05) {
    mtext("*", side = 4, line = 1, cex = 3, las = 1, at = (length(pvals)-n+1))
  }
}

dev.off()
```

## 450k

### Analyses

```{r}
array_450k <- data_ses %>%
  filter(Array_450k == 1)

# associations per generation
# gen 1
gen1_450k <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=array_450k)
# gen 2
gen2_450k <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=array_450k)
# gen 3
gen3_450k <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=array_450k)

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(array_450k,gen1_450k) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(array_450k,gen1_450k)

# associations of DNAm measures
## first gen
#horvath1
horvath1_450k <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=array_450k)

#hannum
hannum_450k <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=array_450k)

#horvath2
horvath2_450k <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=array_450k)

#zhang
zhang_450k <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=array_450k)

#pedbe
pedbe_450k <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_horvath1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=array_450k)

## second gen
#phenoage
phenoage_450k <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=array_450k)

#grimage
grimage_450k <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=array_450k)

## third gen
#dunedinpoam
poam_450k <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=array_450k)

#dunedinpace
pace_450k <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=array_450k)

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(array_450k,horvath1_450k) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(array_450k,horvath1_450k)
```

### Figure

```{r}

#get estimates, lower and higher cis for graphs (full dataset)
ests <- c(coef(gen1_450k)[1], coef(horvath1_450k)[1], coef(hannum_450k)[1], coef(horvath2_450k)[1], coef(zhang_450k)[1], coef(pedbe_450k)[1],coef(gen2_450k)[1], coef(phenoage_450k)[1], coef(grimage_450k)[1], coef(gen3_450k)[1], coef(poam_450k)[1], coef(pace_450k)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1_450k$ci.lb[1], horvath1_450k$ci.lb[1], hannum_450k$ci.lb[1], horvath2_450k$ci.lb[1], zhang_450k$ci.lb[1], pedbe_450k$ci.lb[1], gen2_450k$ci.lb[1], phenoage_450k$ci.lb[1], grimage_450k$ci.lb[1], gen3_450k$ci.lb[1], poam_450k$ci.lb[1], pace_450k$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1_450k$ci.ub[1], horvath1_450k$ci.ub[1], hannum_450k$ci.ub[1], horvath2_450k$ci.ub[1], zhang_450k$ci.ub[1], pedbe_450k$ci.ub[1],gen2_450k$ci.ub[1], phenoage_450k$ci.ub[1], grimage_450k$ci.ub[1], gen3_450k$ci.ub[1], poam_450k$ci.ub[1], pace_450k$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1_450k$pval[1], horvath1_450k$pval[1], hannum_450k$pval[1], horvath2_450k$pval[1], zhang_450k$pval[1], pedbe_450k$pval[1],gen2_450k$pval[1], phenoage_450k$pval[1], grimage_450k$pval[1], gen3_450k$pval[1], poam_450k$pval[1], pace_450k$pval[1])
  
es_no <- c(sum(array_450k$DNAm_gen1), sum(array_450k$DNAm_horvath1), sum(array_450k$DNAm_hannum), 
           sum(array_450k$DNAm_horvath2), sum(array_450k$DNAm_zhang), sum(array_450k$DNAm_pedbe), 
           sum(array_450k$DNAm_gen2), sum(array_450k$DNAm_phenoage), sum(array_450k$DNAm_grimage),
           sum(array_450k$DNAm_gen3), sum(array_450k$DNAm_dunedinpoam), sum(array_450k$DNAm_dunedinpace))


# convert all z's to r
ests <- convert_z2r(ests)
lowcis <- convert_z2r(lowcis)
highcis <- convert_z2r(highcis)


# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Hannum", "Horvath Skin & Blood", "Zhang", "PedBE",
                   "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "3rd Generation", "DunedinPoAm", "DunedinPACE"),           # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no,                        # number of effect sizes
  p_val = round(pvals,2))

png("ses_450k.png", width = 38, height = 25, units = "cm", res = 600)

par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))


# Create the forest plot for the summary effects

forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Pearson's r [95% CI])", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = cbind(summary_effects$ES_no, summary_effects$p_val), ilab.xpos = cbind(-0.39, 0.47),
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 2,
       cex.axis = 1.8,
       textpos = as.numeric(c(-0.7, 0.37)),
       xlim = c(-0.7,0.5),
       alim = c(-0.3,0.1))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits



# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 2.5, par("usr")[2], 3.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.8, 0.6, 1, alpha=0.4))  # Shading for row 3
rect(par("usr")[1], 5.5, par("usr")[2], 6.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.8, 0.6, 1, alpha=0.4))  # Shading for row 6
rect(par("usr")[1], 11.5, par("usr")[2], 12.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.8, 0.6, 1, alpha=0.4))  # Shading for row 12

text(-0.35, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

text(0.45, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "p-value", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

# Add * significant results
for (n in 1:length(pvals)) {
  if (pvals[n] < 0.05) {
    mtext("*", side = 4, line = 1, cex = 3, las = 1, at = (length(pvals)-n+1))
  }
}

title("Association of Socioeconomic Status and DNAm Measures\nof Biological Aging (450k Array)", line = 0, cex.main = 2.5)

dev.off()
```

# Sensitivity Analysis - Cell composition by Tissue

Sensitivity analysis to see if the effects of cell composition differs by tissue.

Will only run this for saliva and venous blood, because there doesn't seem to be enough effect sizes for other tissues (all effect sizes for blood spot are cell composition corrected, only 1 for buccal isn't corrected, and 3 is corrected for cord blood and 3 isn't)

## Venous Blood

For effect sizes using venous blood, run moderator analysis for cell composition for each clock.

There are no effect sizes for PedBE. For Zhang, all effect sizes are cell composition corrected, so will not be included in analysis.

```{r}
blood <- data_ses %>%
  filter(Tissue_blood_ven == 1)

## check for clocks
# horvath1
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=blood[blood$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=blood[blood$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])


# hannum
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=blood[blood$DNAm_hannum == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=blood[blood$DNAm_hannum == 1,])


# horvath2
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=blood[blood$DNAm_horvath2 == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=blood[blood$DNAm_horvath2 == 1,])


# phenoage
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=blood[blood$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=blood[blood$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])

# grimage
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=blood[blood$DNAm_grimage == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=blood[blood$DNAm_grimage == 1,])


# dunedinpoam
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=blood[blood$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=blood[blood$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])


# dunedinpace
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=blood[blood$DNAm_dunedinpace == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=blood[blood$DNAm_dunedinpace == 1,])
```

## Saliva

For effect sizes using saliva, run moderator analysis for cell composition for each clock.

For DunedinPoAm, DunedinPACE, Horvath2 and PedBE, all effect sizes are corrected for cell composition. For GrimAge and PhenoAge only 4 effect sizes corrected for cell composition, so we can only run this for Horvath1 and Hannum.

```{r}
saliva <- data_ses %>%
  filter(Tissue_saliva == 1)

## check for clocks
# horvath1
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=saliva[saliva$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=saliva[saliva$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

# hannum
#accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp_not_acc, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=saliva[saliva$DNAm_hannum == 1,])

#not accounted for as reference
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Cell_comp, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=saliva[saliva$DNAm_hannum == 1,])
```

# Heatmap

Create a heatmap of number of effect sizes per DNAm measure x social determinant of health.

```{r}

#specify orders of variables for the graph
order_dnam <- c("Horvath1", "Hannum", "Horvath2", "Zhang", "PedBE", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "GrimAge2", "DunedinPoAm", "DunedinPACE", "change in Horvath1", "change in Horvath2", "change in Hannum", "change in PedBE", "change in PhenoAge", "change in GrimAge", "change in DunedinPoAm", "change in DunedinPACE")

order_sdoh <- rev(c("Race", "Own education", "Current income/wealth", "Adulthood neighborhood SES", "Own occupational status", "Adulthood SES", "Parental education", "Childhood income/wealth", "Childhood neighborhood SES", "Parental occupational status", "Childhood SES","Change in current income", "Change in adulthood SES", "Change in childhood income", "Occupational status trajectory"))

#update texts in the main data frame so that the labels look nice
for (d in 1:nrow(data)) {
  data$SDOH_time_category[d] <- str_to_sentence(data$SDOH_time_category[d])
  if (grepl("neighborhood ses", data$SDOH_time_category[d])) {
    spl <- strsplit(data$SDOH_time_category[d], split = " ") 
    spl[[1]][3] <- "SES"
    data$SDOH_time_category[d] <- paste(spl[[1]][1], spl[[1]][2], spl[[1]][3], sep = " ")
  } else if (data$SDOH_time_category[d] == "Change in adulthood ses") {
    data$SDOH_time_category[d] <- "Change in adulthood SES"
  } else if (grepl("ses", data$SDOH_time_category[d])) {
    spl <- strsplit(data$SDOH_time_category[d], split = " ") 
    spl[[1]][2] <- "SES"
    data$SDOH_time_category[d] <- paste(spl[[1]][1], spl[[1]][2], sep = " ")}
  }

#count the number of effect sizes for each DNAm measure x SDOH
count_data <- data %>%
  count(DNAm_measure, SDOH_time_category) %>%
  filter(DNAm_measure %in% order_dnam) %>%
  filter(SDOH_time_category %in% order_sdoh) 

# group dnam and sdoh measures into larger categories for the plot (i.e. generations and SES/race categories)
for (c in 1:nrow(count_data)) {
  if (count_data$DNAm_measure[c] %in% c("Horvath1", "Hannum", "Horvath2", "PedBE", "Zhang")) {
    count_data$dnam_grp[c] <- "1st Gen."
    } else if (count_data$DNAm_measure[c] %in% c("PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "GrimAge2")) {
    count_data$dnam_grp[c] <- "2nd Gen."
    } else if (count_data$DNAm_measure[c] %in% c("DunedinPoAm", "DunedinPACE")) {
    count_data$dnam_grp[c] <- "3rd Gen."
    } else if (grepl("change", count_data$DNAm_measure[c])) {
    count_data$dnam_grp[c] <- "Change"}
  if (count_data$SDOH_time_category[c] %in% c("Own education", "Current income/wealth", "Adulthood neighborhood SES", "Own occupational status", "Adulthood SES")) {
    count_data$sdoh_grp[c] <- "Adulthood SES"
    } else if  (count_data$SDOH_time_category[c] %in% c("Parental education", "Childhood income/wealth", "Childhood neighborhood SES", "Parental occupational status", "Childhood SES")) {
    count_data$sdoh_grp[c] <- "Childhood SES"
    } else if (count_data$SDOH_time_category[c] == "Race") {
      count_data$sdoh_grp[c] <- "Race"
    } else if (count_data$SDOH_time_category[c] %in% c("Change in current income", "Change in adulthood SES", "Change in childhood income", "Occupational status trajectory")) {
      count_data$sdoh_grp[c] <- "Change in SES"
  }}

#plot
png("heatmap.png", width = 65, height = 50, units = "cm", res = 600)

ggplot(count_data, aes(x = factor(DNAm_measure, levels = order_dnam), y = factor(SDOH_time_category, levels = order_sdoh), fill = n)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low="lightblue", high="darkblue")  +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept= 0.5:10.5, color="gray20") +
  geom_hline(yintercept= 0.5:11.5, color="gray20") +
  xlab("DNAm Measure") +
  ylab("Social Determinants of Health") +
  labs(fill = "Number of\nEffect Sizes") +
  facet_grid(sdoh_grp ~ dnam_grp, space = "free", scales = "free", switch = "both") +
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 35, color = "black"), axis.text = element_text(size = 30, color = "black"), axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 0.6), legend.text = element_text(size = 30), legend.title = element_text(size = 35), axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.title.y = element_blank(), panel.grid = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(size = 35), strip.placement = "outside", strip.background = element_rect(colour="gray80", fill="gray80"),) 

dev.off()
```


# World map

Create a world map where the countries included in the meta-analysis are colored red, others are gray. The countries that did not contribute individual effect sizes but were included in meta-analyses that were included in our study are also colored red.

```{r}
#load world map
world <- map_data("world") %>%
  mutate(Count = 0)

#count the number of effect sizes per country
countries <- data %>%
  count(Country)

#check if the country is included in our dataset 
for (c in 1:nrow(countries)) {
  for (w in 1:nrow(world)) {
    if (countries$Country[c] == world$region[w] || grepl(world$region[w], countries$Country[c])){
      world$Count[w] <- 1
    }
  }
}

#specify the colors for the plot (gray and red)
for (w in 1:nrow(world)) {
  if (world$Count[w] == 1) {world$Color[w] = "brown2"}
  else {world$Color[w] = "gray50"}
  
}

#plot
png("worldmap.png", width = 75, height = 50, units = "cm", res = 600)

ggplot(data = world) +
  geom_map(map = world, aes(long, lat, map_id = region, fill = Color)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray50" = "gray50", "brown2" = "brown2")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank(), axis.text = element_blank(), panel.grid = element_blank(), legend.position = "none")

dev.off()
```

# Correlation figure


```{r}

library(tidyverse)

# 1) Data
df <- tribble(
  ~Generation,           ~SES,   ~SES_sig, ~Black_vs_White, ~BW_sig, ~Latinx_vs_White, ~Latinx_sig,
  "First generation",    -0.03,  TRUE,     -0.03,           FALSE,   0.04,             FALSE,
  "Horvath1",            -0.01,  FALSE,    -0.01,           FALSE,  -0.01,             FALSE,
  "Hannum",              -0.03,  TRUE,     -0.06,           TRUE,    0.11,             TRUE,
  "Horvath2",            -0.04,  TRUE,      0.04,           FALSE,   0.08,             FALSE,
  "Zhang",               -0.04,  FALSE,    -0.27,           TRUE,     NA,              FALSE,
  "PedBE",               -0.02,  FALSE,      NA,            FALSE,    NA,              FALSE,
  "Second generation",   -0.11,  TRUE,      0.14,           TRUE,    0.04,             FALSE,
  "PhenoAge",            -0.07,  TRUE,      0.11,           TRUE,    0.06,             FALSE,
  "GrimAge",             -0.14,  TRUE,      0.17,           TRUE,    0.05,             FALSE,
  "Third generation",    -0.13,  TRUE,      0.20,           TRUE,    0.13,             TRUE,
  "DunedinPoAm",         -0.13,  TRUE,      0.19,           TRUE,    0.10,             TRUE,
  "DunedinPACE",         -0.15,  TRUE,      0.22,           TRUE,    0.17,             TRUE
)

# 2) Keep table order exactly as written
df$Generation <- factor(df$Generation, levels = c(
  "First generation","Horvath1","Hannum","Horvath2","Zhang","PedBE",
  "Second generation","PhenoAge","GrimAge",
  "Third generation","DunedinPoAm","DunedinPACE"
))
x_levels <- c("SES","Black_vs_White","Latinx_vs_White")

# 3) Reshape data to long format
vals_long <- df %>%
  select(Generation, all_of(x_levels)) %>%
  pivot_longer(-Generation, names_to = "Variable", values_to = "r") %>%
  mutate(Variable = factor(Variable, levels = x_levels))

sigs_long <- df %>%
  select(Generation, SES_sig, BW_sig, Latinx_sig) %>%
  pivot_longer(-Generation, names_to = "sig_var", values_to = "sig") %>%
  mutate(Variable = recode(sig_var,
                           SES_sig = "SES",
                           BW_sig = "Black_vs_White",
                           Latinx_sig = "Latinx_vs_White")) %>%
  select(-sig_var) %>%
  mutate(Variable = factor(Variable, levels = x_levels))

df_long <- vals_long %>%
  left_join(sigs_long, by = c("Generation","Variable")) %>%
  mutate(
    label = ifelse(is.na(r), "", sprintf("%.2f%s", r, ifelse(sig, " *", ""))),
    txt_col = ifelse(!is.na(r) & abs(r) > 0.40, "white", "black")
  )

# 4) Plot with correct top-down order
p <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Variable, y = Generation, fill = r)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = label, color = txt_col), size = 4.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_identity() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red",
    midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1), na.value = "grey85",
    name = "Pearson’s r"
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(levels(df$Generation))) +   # << reverse order
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        panel.grid = element_blank()) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL)

print(p)

dev.off()
```

# Descriptives

```{r}
### child-child descriptives ###
data_ses_child <- data_ses %>%
  filter(SES_time == 3)

# number of effect sizes: 113
nrow(data_ses_child)

# countries and the number of effect sizes form them
# max: USA with 60, UK with 34 effect sizes
data_ses_child %>%
  count(Country)

# age descriptives
# mean age: 10.07
mean(data_ses_child$Age_DNAm_Mean)

# range: 0-18
min(data_ses_child$Age_DNAm_Mean)
max(data_ses_child$Age_DNAm_Mean)

# sex distribution
data_ses_child %>%
  count(Sex)

# tissue count with percentages
# saliva: 42 effect sizes, 37%; venous blood: 60 effect sizes, 53%
data_ses_child %>%
  count(Tissue) %>%
  mutate(percent = round(n/nrow(data_ses_child)*100))

#----------------------------------------------------------------------
### child-adult descriptives ###
data_ses_child_adult <- data_ses %>%
  filter(SES_time == 2)

# number of effect sizes: 173
nrow(data_ses_child_adult)

# countries and the number of effect sizes form them
# max: USA with 145, Switzerland with 12 effect sizes
data_ses_child_adult %>%
  count(Country)

# age descriptives
# mean age: 56.7
mean(data_ses_child_adult$Age_DNAm_Mean)

# range: 20-79
min(data_ses_child_adult$Age_DNAm_Mean)
max(data_ses_child_adult$Age_DNAm_Mean)

# tissue count with percentages
# venous blood: 146 effect sizes, 84%; saliva: 13 effect sizes, 8%
data_ses_child_adult %>%
  count(Tissue) %>%
  mutate(percent = round(n/nrow(data_ses_child_adult)*100))

#----------------------------------------------------------------------
### adult-adult descriptives ###
data_ses_adult <- data_ses %>%
  filter(SES_time == 1)

# number of effect sizes: 622
nrow(data_ses_adult)

# countries and the number of effect sizes form them
# max: USA with 427, UK with 42 effect sizes
data_ses_adult %>%
  count(Country)

# age descriptives
# mean age: 55.75
mean(data_ses_adult$Age_DNAm_Mean)

# range: 19.3-85.8
min(data_ses_adult$Age_DNAm_Mean)
max(data_ses_adult$Age_DNAm_Mean)

# tissue count with percentages
# venous blood: 551 effect sizes, 89%; blood spot: 52 effect sizes, 8%; saliva: 11 effect sizes, 2%
data_ses_adult %>%
count(Tissue) %>%
  mutate(percent = round(n/nrow(data_ses_adult)*100))

#----------------------------------------------------------------------
### black vs white descriptives ###

black <- data_race %>%
  filter(Race_category == "Black") 

# country: only USA
unique(black$Country)

# number of effect sizes: 68
nrow(black)

# age descriptives
# mean age: 48.4
mean(black$Age_DNAm_Mean)

# range: 3.4-85.8
min(black$Age_DNAm_Mean)
max(black$Age_DNAm_Mean)

# how many from kids?
# 2 from early childhood, 2 from late childhood, 10 from adolescence
# 12 from young adult, 13 from mid adult, 29 from old adult
table(black$Age_DNAm_categ)

# black vs white #
black <- data_race %>%
  filter(Race_category == "Black") 

# age descriptives
# mean age: 48.4
mean(black$Age_DNAm_Mean)

# range: 3.4-85.8
min(black$Age_DNAm_Mean)
max(black$Age_DNAm_Mean)

# how many from kids?
# 2 from early childhood, 2 from late childhood, 10 from adolescence
# 12 from young adult, 13 from mid adult, 29 from old adult
table(black$Age_DNAm_categ)


#----------------------------------------------------------------------
### latinx vs white descriptives ###

latinx <- data_race %>%
  filter(Race_category == "Latinx") 

# country: only USA
unique(latinx$Country)

# number of effect sizes: 41
nrow(latinx)

# age descriptives
# mean age: 46.9
mean(latinx$Age_DNAm_Mean)

# range: 3.4-85.8
min(latinx$Age_DNAm_Mean)
max(latinx$Age_DNAm_Mean)

# how many from kids?
# 2 from early childhood, 2 from late childhood, 11 from adolescence
# 4 from mid adult, 22 from old adult
table(latinx$Age_DNAm_categ)

#----------------------------------------------------------------------
### covariates descriptives ###
table(data$Covariates_num)
median(data$Covariates_num) #8
mean(data$Covariates_num) #9.78
sum(data$Covariates_morethan5) #643
sum(data$Covariates_2to5) #189
sum(data$Covariates_lessthan2) #233

#count number of some key covariates
sum(grepl("age",data$Covariates)) #564
sum(grepl("sex",data$Covariates)) #638
sum(grepl("smok",data$Covariates)) #420
sum(grepl("bmi",data$Covariates)) #18
sum(grepl("BMI",data$Covariates)) #330

```

# Covariate analyses
Check for each DNAm measure, whether having >5 covariates or controlling for smoking or BMI affect the results

## <= or > 5 covatiates

Only ran for SES, as Black vs White or Latinx vs White did not have at least 5 effect sizes per category to satisfy the moderator analysis conditions.

Significant difference only for DunedinPoAm. Not enough effect sizes to run analysis on Zhang.
```{r}
## horvath 1
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_morethan5, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

## hannum
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_morethan5, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_hannum == 1,])

## horvath 2
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_morethan5, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath2 == 1,])

## pedbe
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_morethan5, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_pedbe == 1,])

## phenoage
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_morethan5, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])

## grimage
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_morethan5, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,])

## dunedinpoam
### reference: less than or equal to 5 covariates
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_morethan5, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])
### reference: more than 5 covariates
poam_covnum <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_lessthan_equal5, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])
#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation analyses
variance_distribution(data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,],poam_covnum) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,],poam_covnum)

## dunedinpace
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_morethan5, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpace == 1,])
```


## BMI

### SES
Not enough effect sizes for PedBE and Zhang, so they are not included in moderator analyses here. Signfificant difference for GrimAge and DunedinPoAm.
```{r}
## horvath 1
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_bmi, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

## hannum
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_bmi, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_hannum == 1,])

## horvath 2
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_bmi, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath2 == 1,])

## phenoage
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_bmi, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])

## grimage
### reference: BMI included as a covariate
```
