```{r}
grim_bmi <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_bmi, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,])
### reference: BMI not included as a covariate
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_bmi, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,])
#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation analyses
variance_distribution(data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,],grim_bmi) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,],grim_bmi)

## dunedinpoam
### reference: BMI included as a covariate
poam_bmi <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_bmi, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])
### reference: BMI not included as a covariate
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_bmi, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])
#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation analyses
variance_distribution(data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,],poam_bmi) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,],poam_bmi)

## dunedinpace
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_bmi, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpace == 1,])
```

### Race
Not enough effect sizes in Latinx vs White dataset for any DNAm measures. For Black vs White, enough effect sizes are present only for Horvath1 and Hannum, and neither reveal significant results.

```{r}
## horvath 1
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_bmi, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black[black$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

## hannum
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_bmi, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black[black$DNAm_hannum == 1,])
```


## Smoking

### SES
Not enough effect sizes for PedBE and Zhang, so they are not included in moderator analyses here. Significant results for GrimAge and DunedinPoAm 
```{r}
## horvath 1
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_smoking, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

## hannum
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_smoking, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_hannum == 1,])

## horvath 2
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_smoking, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_horvath2 == 1,])

## phenoage
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_smoking, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])

## grimage
### reference: Smoking included as a covariate
grim_smoking <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_smoking, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,])
### reference: Smoking not included as a covariate
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_smoking, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,])
#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation analyses
variance_distribution(data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,],grim_smoking) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_grimage == 1,],grim_smoking)

## dunedinpoam
### reference: Smoking included as a covariate
poam_smoking <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_smoking, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])
### reference: Smoking not included as a covariate
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_smoking, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,])
#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation analyses
variance_distribution(data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,],poam_smoking) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam == 1,],poam_smoking)

## dunedinpace
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_smoking, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses[data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpace == 1,])
```

### Race
Not enough effect sizes in Latinx vs White dataset for any DNAm measures. 

For Black vs White, enough effect sizes are present only for Horvath1, Hannum, PhenoAge and GrimAge.
Significant difference for Hannum, but only 5 and 6 effect sizes per category.
```{r}
## horvath 1
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_smoking, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black[black$DNAm_horvath1 == 1,])

## hannum
### reference: Smoking included as a covariate (6 effect sizes)
hannum_smoking <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_smoking, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black[black$DNAm_hannum == 1,])
### reference: Smoking not included as a covariate (5 effect sizes)
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_smoking, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black[black$DNAm_hannum == 1,])
#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation analyses
variance_distribution(black[black$DNAm_hannum == 1,],hannum_smoking) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(black[black$DNAm_hannum == 1,],hannum_smoking)

## phenoage
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_smoking, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black[black$DNAm_phenoage == 1,])

## grimage
rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ Covariates_no_smoking, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black[black$DNAm_grimage == 1,])
```

# Leave-One-Cohort-Out Sensitivity Analyses

In a loop, remove 20 influential cohorts (contributing more than 3 studies or 25 effect sizes) one by one and re-run the analyses to see if the results hold.

Run the loops separately for SES and race, as only 5 cohorts contribute to Black vs White effect sizes, and 4 contribute to Latinx vs White effect sizes.

## SES
```{r}
data_ses_all <- data_ses

cohorts <- c("Health and Retirement Study", "Project Viva", "Women's Health Initiative", "Birmingham Youth Violence Study", "Texas Twin Project", "Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents and Children", "Canadian Longitudinal Study on Aging", "E-Risk", "Family and Community Health Study", "Female Growth and Development Study", "Finnish Twin Cohort", "Generation Scotland", "Lothian Birth Cohort 1936", "Multi-Ethnic Study of Atherosclerosis", "MRC National Survey of Health and Development", "The Irish Longitudinal Study on Ageing", "Understanding Society", "Coronary Artery Risk Development in Young Adults", "My Body My Story", "Swiss Kidney Project on Genes in Hypertension")

for (c in 1:length(cohorts)) {
### prepare data ### 
data_ses <- data_ses_all %>%
  filter(Cohort != cohorts[c])

png_name <- paste0("ses_no_",regex(cohorts[c])[1], ".png")

plot_title <- paste0("Association of Socioeconomic Status and DNAm Measures\nof Biological Aging - Excluding ", cohorts[c])
  
### run generation analyses ### 
#gen 1
gen1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

#gen 2
gen2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

#gen 3
gen3 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

### run dnam analyses ### 
#horvath1
horvath1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

#hannum
hannum <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

#horvath2
horvath2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

#zhang
zhang <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

#pedbe
pedbe <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_horvath1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

#phenoage
phenoage <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

#grimage
grimage <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

#dunedinpoam
poam <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

#dunedinpace
pace <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses)

### plot figure ### 
#get estimates, lower and higher cis for graphs (full dataset)
ests <- c(coef(gen1)[1], coef(horvath1)[1], coef(hannum)[1], coef(horvath2)[1], coef(zhang)[1], coef(pedbe)[1], 
          coef(gen2)[1], coef(phenoage)[1], coef(grimage)[1], coef(gen3)[1], coef(poam)[1], coef(pace)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1$ci.lb[1], horvath1$ci.lb[1], hannum$ci.lb[1], horvath2$ci.lb[1], zhang$ci.lb[1], pedbe$ci.lb[1],
            gen2$ci.lb[1], phenoage$ci.lb[1], grimage$ci.lb[1], gen3$ci.lb[1], poam$ci.lb[1], pace$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1$ci.ub[1], horvath1$ci.ub[1], hannum$ci.ub[1], horvath2$ci.ub[1], zhang$ci.ub[1], pedbe$ci.ub[1],
            gen2$ci.ub[1], phenoage$ci.ub[1], grimage$ci.ub[1], gen3$ci.ub[1], poam$ci.ub[1], pace$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1$pval[1], horvath1$pval[1], hannum$pval[1], horvath2$pval[1], zhang$pval[1], pedbe$pval[1],
            gen2$pval[1], phenoage$pval[1], grimage$pval[1], gen3$pval[1], poam$pval[1], pace$pval[1])
  
es_no <- c(sum(data_ses$DNAm_gen1), sum(data_ses$DNAm_horvath1), sum(data_ses$DNAm_hannum), 
           sum(data_ses$DNAm_horvath2), sum(data_ses$DNAm_zhang), sum(data_ses$DNAm_pedbe), 
           sum(data_ses$DNAm_gen2), sum(data_ses$DNAm_phenoage), sum(data_ses$DNAm_grimage),
           sum(data_ses$DNAm_gen3), sum(data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpoam), sum(data_ses$DNAm_dunedinpace))


# convert all z's to r
ests <- convert_z2r(ests)
lowcis <- convert_z2r(lowcis)
highcis <- convert_z2r(highcis)


# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Hannum", "Horvath Skin & Blood", "Zhang", "PedBE",
                   "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "3rd Generation", "DunedinPoAm", "DunedinPACE"),           # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no,                        # number of effect sizes
  p_val = round(pvals,2))

png(png_name, width = 38, height = 25, units = "cm", res = 600)

par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))

# Create the forest plot for the summary effects

forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Pearson's r [95% CI])", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = cbind(summary_effects$ES_no, summary_effects$p_val), ilab.xpos = cbind(-0.3, 0.47),
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 2,
       cex.axis = 1.8,
       textpos = as.numeric(c(-0.6, 0.37)),
       xlim = c(-0.6,0.5),
       alim = c(-0.25,0.15))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits

# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 2.5, par("usr")[2], 3.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 3
rect(par("usr")[1], 5.5, par("usr")[2], 6.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 6
rect(par("usr")[1], 11.5, par("usr")[2], 12.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 12

text(0.45, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "p-value", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

text(-0.29, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label


title(plot_title, line = 0, cex.main = 2.5)

# Add * significant results
for (n in 1:length(pvals)) {
  if (pvals[n] < 0.05) {
    mtext("*", side = 4, line = 1, cex = 3, las = 1, at = (length(pvals)-n+1))
  }
}
dev.off()
}
```

## Black vs White

```{r}
cohorts <- c("Health and Retirement Study", "Project Viva", "Women's Health Initiative", "Birmingham Youth Violence Study", "Texas Twin Project")

for (c in 1:length(cohorts)) {
### prepare data ### 
black <- data_race %>%
  filter(Race_category == "Black") %>%
  filter(Cohort != cohorts[c])
  
png_name <- paste0("black_no_",regex(cohorts[c])[1], ".png")


plot_title <- paste("Black- Compared to White-Identifying Groups\nExcluding", cohorts[c])

### run generation analyses ### 
# gen 1
gen1_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

# gen 2
gen2_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

# gen 3
gen3_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)


### run dnam analyses ### 
#horvath1
horvath1_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

#hannum
hannum_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)


#horvath2
horvath2_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

#zhang
zhang_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_horvath1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

#phenoage
phenoage_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

#grimage
grimage_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

#dunedinpoam
poam_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

#dunedinpace
pace_black <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=black)

### plot figure ### 
#get estimates, lower and higher cis for graphs (white vs black)
ests <- c(coef(gen1_black)[1], coef(horvath1_black)[1], coef(hannum_black)[1], coef(horvath2_black)[1], 
          coef(zhang_black)[1], coef(gen2_black)[1], coef(phenoage_black)[1], coef(grimage_black)[1], 
          coef(gen3_black)[1], coef(poam_black)[1], coef(pace_black)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1_black$ci.lb[1], horvath1_black$ci.lb[1], hannum_black$ci.lb[1], horvath2_black$ci.lb[1], 
            zhang_black$ci.lb[1], gen2_black$ci.lb[1], phenoage_black$ci.lb[1], grimage_black$ci.lb[1], 
            gen3_black$ci.lb[1], poam_black$ci.lb[1], pace_black$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1_black$ci.ub[1], horvath1_black$ci.ub[1], hannum_black$ci.ub[1], horvath2_black$ci.ub[1], 
            zhang_black$ci.ub[1], gen2_black$ci.ub[1], phenoage_black$ci.ub[1], grimage_black$ci.ub[1], 
            gen3_black$ci.ub[1], poam_black$ci.ub[1], pace_black$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1_black$pval[1], horvath1_black$pval[1], hannum_black$pval[1], horvath2_black$pval[1], 
           zhang_black$pval[1], gen2_black$pval[1], phenoage_black$pval[1], grimage_black$pval[1], 
           gen3_black$pval[1], poam_black$pval[1], pace_black$pval[1])
  
es_no <- c(sum(black$DNAm_gen1), sum(black$DNAm_horvath1), sum(black$DNAm_hannum), sum(black$DNAm_horvath2),
          sum(black$DNAm_zhang), sum(black$DNAm_gen2), sum(black$DNAm_phenoage), sum(black$DNAm_grimage),
           sum(black$DNAm_gen3), sum(black$DNAm_dunedinpoam), sum(black$DNAm_dunedinpace))


# convert all z's to r
ests <- convert_z2r(ests)
lowcis <- convert_z2r(lowcis)
highcis <- convert_z2r(highcis)

# convert all r's to d
ests <- r_to_d(ests)
lowcis <- r_to_d(lowcis)
highcis <- r_to_d(highcis)

png(png_name, width = 38, height = 25, units = "cm", res = 600)

par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))

# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Hannum", "Horvath Skin & Blood", "Zhang",
                   "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "3rd Generation", "DunedinPoAm", "DunedinPACE"),           # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no,                        # number of effect sizes
  p_val = round(pvals,2))

# Create the forest plot for the summary effects

forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Cohen's d [95% CI])", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = cbind(summary_effects$ES_no, summary_effects$p_val), ilab.xpos = cbind(-1.22, 1.75),
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 2,
       cex.axis = 1.8,
       textpos = as.numeric(c(-2.3, 1.5)),
       xlim = c(-2.3,1.9),
       alim = c(-1.1,0.7))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits


# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 2.5, par("usr")[2], 3.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.4, 0.7, 1, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 3
rect(par("usr")[1], 5.5, par("usr")[2], 6.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.4, 0.7, 1, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 6
rect(par("usr")[1], 10.5, par("usr")[2], 11.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.4, 0.7, 1, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 12

text(-1, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2.5)  # Adjust position of the label

text(1.72, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "p-value", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

title(plot_title, line = 0, cex.main = 2)

# Add * significant results
for (n in 1:length(pvals)) {
  if (pvals[n] < 0.05) {
    mtext("*", side = 4, line = 1, cex = 3, las = 1, at = (length(pvals)-n+1))
  }
}
dev.off()
}
```

## Latinx vs White

```{r}
cohorts <- c("Health and Retirement Study", "Project Viva", "Women's Health Initiative", "Texas Twin Project")


for (c in 1:length(cohorts)) {
### prepare data ### 
latinx <- data_race %>%
  filter(Race_category == "Latinx") %>%
  filter(Cohort != cohorts[c])
  
png_name <- paste0("latinx_no_",regex(cohorts[c])[1], ".png")


plot_title <- paste("Latinx- Compared to White-Identifying Groups\nExcluding", cohorts[c])

### run generation analyses ### 
# gen 1
gen1_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)

# gen 2
gen2_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)

# gen 3
gen3_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)

### run dnam analyses ### 
## first gen
#horvath1
horvath1_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)


#hannum
hannum_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)

#horvath2
horvath2_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)

## second gen
#phenoage
phenoage_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam , random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)

#grimage
grimage_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)

## third gen
#dunedinpoam
poam_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_horvath1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)

#dunedinpace
pace_latinx <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=latinx)


### plot figure ### 
#get estimates, lower and higher cis for graphs (white vs black)
ests <- c(coef(gen1_latinx)[1], coef(horvath1_latinx)[1], coef(hannum_latinx)[1], coef(horvath2_latinx)[1], 
          coef(gen2_latinx)[1], coef(phenoage_latinx)[1], coef(grimage_latinx)[1], coef(gen3_latinx)[1], 
          coef(poam_latinx)[1], coef(pace_latinx)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1_latinx$ci.lb[1], horvath1_latinx$ci.lb[1], hannum_latinx$ci.lb[1], horvath2_latinx$ci.lb[1], 
            gen2_latinx$ci.lb[1], phenoage_latinx$ci.lb[1], grimage_latinx$ci.lb[1], gen3_latinx$ci.lb[1], 
            poam_latinx$ci.lb[1], pace_latinx$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1_latinx$ci.ub[1], horvath1_latinx$ci.ub[1], hannum_latinx$ci.ub[1], horvath2_latinx$ci.ub[1], 
            gen2_latinx$ci.ub[1], phenoage_latinx$ci.ub[1], grimage_latinx$ci.ub[1], gen3_latinx$ci.ub[1], 
            poam_latinx$ci.ub[1], pace_latinx$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1_latinx$pval[1], horvath1_latinx$pval[1], hannum_latinx$pval[1], horvath2_latinx$pval[1], 
            gen2_latinx$pval[1], phenoage_latinx$pval[1], grimage_latinx$pval[1], gen3_latinx$pval[1], 
           poam_latinx$pval[1], pace_latinx$pval[1])
  
es_no <- c(sum(latinx$DNAm_gen1), sum(latinx$DNAm_horvath1), sum(latinx$DNAm_hannum), sum(latinx$DNAm_horvath2),
          sum(latinx$DNAm_gen2), sum(latinx$DNAm_phenoage), sum(latinx$DNAm_grimage),
           sum(latinx$DNAm_gen3), sum(latinx$DNAm_dunedinpoam), sum(latinx$DNAm_dunedinpace))


# convert all z's to r
ests <- convert_z2r(ests)
lowcis <- convert_z2r(lowcis)
highcis <- convert_z2r(highcis)

# convert all r's to d
ests <- r_to_d(ests)
lowcis <- r_to_d(lowcis)
highcis <- r_to_d(highcis)

png(png_name, width = 38, height = 23, units = "cm", res = 600)
par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))

# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Hannum", "Horvath Skin & Blood",
                   "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "3rd Generation", "DunedinPoAm", "DunedinPACE"),           # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no,                        # number of effect sizes
  p_val = round(pvals,2))

# Create the forest plot for the summary effects

forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Cohen's d [95% CI])", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = cbind(summary_effects$ES_no, summary_effects$p_val), ilab.xpos = cbind(-1.22, 1.75),
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 2,
       cex.axis = 1.8,
       textpos = as.numeric(c(-2.3, 1.5)),
       xlim = c(-2.3,1.9),
       alim = c(-1.1,0.7))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits


# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 2.5, par("usr")[2], 3.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.4, 0.7, 1, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 3
rect(par("usr")[1], 5.5, par("usr")[2], 6.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.4, 0.7, 1, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 6
rect(par("usr")[1], 9.5, par("usr")[2], 10.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.4, 0.7, 1, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 12

text(-1, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2.5)  # Adjust position of the label

text(1.72, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "p-value", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label

title(plot_title, line = 0, cex.main = 2)

# Add * significant results
for (n in 1:length(pvals)) {
  if (pvals[n] < 0.05) {
    mtext("*", side = 4, line = 1, cex = 3, las = 1, at = (length(pvals)-n+1))
  }
}

dev.off()
}
```

# DNAm Time x Tissue

For adulthood DNAm-adulthood SES and for childhood DNAm-childhood SES, run generation- and clock-level meta-analyses separately for effect sizes from venous blood and saliva.

## Adulthood DNAm - Adulthood SES

### Blood
```{r}
# prepare data
data_ses1_bl <- data_ses %>%
  filter(SES_time1 == 1) %>%
  filter(Tissue_blood_ven == 1)

# associations per generation
# gen 1
gen1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses1_bl)

# gen 2
gen2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses1_bl)

# gen 3
gen3 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses1_bl)

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(data_ses1_bl,gen1) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses1_bl,gen1)

# associations of DNAm measures
## first gen
#horvath1
horvath1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses1_bl)

#hannum
hannum <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses1_bl)

#horvath2
horvath2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses1_bl)

#zhang
zhang <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_horvath1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses1_bl)

## second gen
#phenoage
phenoage <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses1_bl)

#grimage
grimage <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses1_bl)

## third gen
#dunedinpoam
poam <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses1_bl)

#dunedinpace
pace <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_zhang, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses1_bl)

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for DNAm-level analyses (since the meta-analytic model is the same for all DNAm measures, running this once should be enough)
variance_distribution(data_ses1_bl,horvath1) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses1_bl,horvath1)
```

```{r}
#get estimates, lower and higher cis for graphs (adulthood ses-adulthood dnam dataset)
ests <- c(coef(gen1)[1], coef(horvath1)[1], coef(hannum)[1], coef(horvath2)[1],
          coef(zhang)[1], coef(gen2)[1], coef(phenoage)[1],
          coef(grimage)[1], coef(gen3)[1], coef(poam)[1], coef(pace)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1$ci.lb[1], horvath1$ci.lb[1], hannum$ci.lb[1], horvath2$ci.lb[1], 
            zhang$ci.lb[1], gen2$ci.lb[1], phenoage$ci.lb[1], 
            grimage$ci.lb[1], gen3$ci.lb[1], poam$ci.lb[1], pace$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1$ci.ub[1], horvath1$ci.ub[1], hannum$ci.ub[1], horvath2$ci.ub[1], 
             zhang$ci.ub[1], gen2$ci.ub[1], phenoage$ci.ub[1], 
             grimage$ci.ub[1], gen3$ci.ub[1], poam$ci.ub[1], pace$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1$pval[1], horvath1$pval[1], hannum$pval[1], horvath2$pval[1], zhang$pval[1], 
           gen2$pval[1], phenoage$pval[1], grimage$pval[1], gen3$pval[1], 
           poam$pval[1], pace$pval[1])
  
es_no <- c(sum(data_ses1_bl$DNAm_gen1), sum(data_ses1_bl$DNAm_horvath1), 
           sum(data_ses1_bl$DNAm_hannum),sum(data_ses1_bl$DNAm_horvath2),
          sum(data_ses1_bl$DNAm_zhang), sum(data_ses1_bl$DNAm_gen2), 
          sum(data_ses1_bl$DNAm_phenoage),sum(data_ses1_bl$DNAm_grimage),
          sum(data_ses1_bl$DNAm_gen3),sum(data_ses1_bl$DNAm_dunedinpoam),
          sum(data_ses1_bl$DNAm_dunedinpace))


# convert all z's to r
ests <- convert_z2r(ests)
lowcis <- convert_z2r(lowcis)
highcis <- convert_z2r(highcis)


# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Hannum", "Horvath Skin & Blood", "Zhang",
                   "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "3rd Generation", "DunedinPoAm", "DunedinPACE"),           # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no                        # number of effect sizes
  )

png("adult_blood.png", width = 38, height = 25, units = "cm", res = 600)

par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))

# Create the forest plot for the summary effects

forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Pearson's r)", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = summary_effects$ES_no, ilab.xpos = -0.37,
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 2,
       cex.axis = 1.8,
       xlim = c(-0.65,0.45),
       alim = c(-0.35,0.2))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits


# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 2.5, par("usr")[2], 3.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 3
rect(par("usr")[1], 5.5, par("usr")[2], 6.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 6
rect(par("usr")[1], 10.5, par("usr")[2], 11.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 11


text(-0.36, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label


title("Association of Adulthood SES and DNAm Measures\nof Biological Aging Measured During Adulthood in Venous Blood", line = 0, cex.main = 2.5)

# Add * significant results
for (n in 1:length(pvals)) {
  if (pvals[n] < 0.05) {
    mtext("*", side = 4, line = 1, cex = 3, las = 1, at = (length(pvals)-n+1))
  }
}

dev.off()
```

### Saliva
```{r}
# prepare data
data_ses1_sal <- data_ses %>%
  filter(SES_time1 == 1) %>%
  filter(Tissue_saliva == 1)

# associations per generation
# gen 1
gen1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses1_sal)

# gen 2
gen2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses1_sal)


#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(data_ses1_sal,gen1) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses1_sal,gen1)

# associations of DNAm measures
## first gen
#horvath1
horvath1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses1_sal)

#hannum
hannum <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_phenoage, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses1_sal)

## second gen
#phenoage
phenoage <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses1_sal)

#grimage
grimage <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses1_sal)


#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for DNAm-level analyses (since the meta-analytic model is the same for all DNAm measures, running this once should be enough)
variance_distribution(data_ses1_sal,horvath1) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses1_sal,horvath1)
```

```{r}
#get estimates, lower and higher cis for graphs (adulthood ses-adulthood dnam dataset)
ests <- c(coef(gen1)[1], coef(horvath1)[1], coef(hannum)[1], 
          coef(gen2)[1], coef(phenoage)[1],coef(grimage)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1$ci.lb[1], horvath1$ci.lb[1], hannum$ci.lb[1], gen2$ci.lb[1],
            phenoage$ci.lb[1], grimage$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1$ci.ub[1], horvath1$ci.ub[1], hannum$ci.ub[1], gen2$ci.ub[1],
             phenoage$ci.ub[1],grimage$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1$pval[1], horvath1$pval[1], hannum$pval[1],
           gen2$pval[1], phenoage$pval[1], grimage$pval[1])
  
es_no <- c(sum(data_ses1_sal$DNAm_gen1), sum(data_ses1_sal$DNAm_horvath1), 
           sum(data_ses1_sal$DNAm_hannum), sum(data_ses1_sal$DNAm_gen2), 
          sum(data_ses1_sal$DNAm_phenoage),sum(data_ses1_sal$DNAm_grimage))


# convert all z's to r
ests <- convert_z2r(ests)
lowcis <- convert_z2r(lowcis)
highcis <- convert_z2r(highcis)


# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Hannum",
                   "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge"),           # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no                        # number of effect sizes
  )

png("adult_saliva.png", width = 38, height = 25, units = "cm", res = 600)

par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))

# Create the forest plot for the summary effects

forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Pearson's r)", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = summary_effects$ES_no, ilab.xpos = -0.37,
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 2,
       cex.axis = 1.8,
       xlim = c(-0.65,0.45),
       alim = c(-0.35,0.2))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits


# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 2.5, par("usr")[2], 3.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 3
rect(par("usr")[1], 5.5, par("usr")[2], 6.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 6


text(-0.36, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label


title("Association of Adulthood SES and DNAm Measures\nof Biological Aging Measured During Adulthood in Saliva", line = 0, cex.main = 2.5)

# Add * significant results
for (n in 1:length(pvals)) {
  if (pvals[n] < 0.05) {
    mtext("*", side = 4, line = 1, cex = 3, las = 1, at = (length(pvals)-n+1))
  }
}

dev.off()
```


## Childhood DNAm - Childhood SES

### Blood
```{r}
# prepare data
data_ses3_bl <- data_ses %>%
  filter(SES_time3 == 1) %>%
  filter(Tissue_blood_ven == 1)

# associations per generation
# gen 1
gen1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_bl)

# gen 2
gen2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_bl)

# gen 3
gen3 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_bl)

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(data_ses3_bl,gen1) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses3_bl,gen1)

# associations of DNAm measures
## first gen
#horvath1
horvath1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_bl)

#hannum
hannum <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_bl)

#horvath2
horvath2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_bl)

## second gen
#phenoage
phenoage <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_bl)

#grimage
grimage <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_bl)

## third gen
#dunedinpoam
poam <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_horvath1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_bl)

#dunedinpace
pace <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpoam, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_bl)

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for DNAm-level analyses (since the meta-analytic model is the same for all DNAm measures, running this once should be enough)
variance_distribution(data_ses3_bl,horvath1) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses3_bl,horvath1)
```

```{r}
#get estimates, lower and higher cis for graphs (full dataset)
ests <- c(coef(gen1)[1], coef(horvath1)[1], coef(hannum)[1], coef(horvath2)[1], 
          coef(gen2)[1], coef(phenoage)[1], coef(grimage)[1], coef(gen3)[1], coef(poam)[1], coef(pace)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1$ci.lb[1], horvath1$ci.lb[1], hannum$ci.lb[1], horvath2$ci.lb[1],
            gen2$ci.lb[1], phenoage$ci.lb[1], grimage$ci.lb[1], gen3$ci.lb[1], poam$ci.lb[1], pace$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1$ci.ub[1], horvath1$ci.ub[1], hannum$ci.ub[1], horvath2$ci.ub[1],
            gen2$ci.ub[1], phenoage$ci.ub[1], grimage$ci.ub[1], gen3$ci.ub[1], poam$ci.ub[1], pace$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1$pval[1], horvath1$pval[1], hannum$pval[1], horvath2$pval[1],
            gen2$pval[1], phenoage$pval[1], grimage$pval[1], gen3$pval[1], poam$pval[1], pace$pval[1])
  
es_no <- c(sum(data_ses3_bl$DNAm_gen1), sum(data_ses3_bl$DNAm_horvath1), sum(data_ses3_bl$DNAm_hannum), 
           sum(data_ses3_bl$DNAm_horvath2),sum(data_ses3_bl$DNAm_gen2), sum(data_ses3_bl$DNAm_phenoage), 
           sum(data_ses3_bl$DNAm_grimage),sum(data_ses3_bl$DNAm_gen3), sum(data_ses3_bl$DNAm_dunedinpoam), 
           sum(data_ses3_bl$DNAm_dunedinpace))


# convert all z's to r
ests <- convert_z2r(ests)
lowcis <- convert_z2r(lowcis)
highcis <- convert_z2r(highcis)


# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Hannum", "Horvath Skin & Blood",
                   "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "3rd Generation", "DunedinPoAm", "DunedinPACE"),           # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no                        # number of effect sizes
  )

png("childhood_blood.png", width = 38, height = 25, units = "cm", res = 600)

par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))

# Create the forest plot for the summary effects

forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Pearson's r)", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = summary_effects$ES_no, ilab.xpos = -0.37,
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 2,
       cex.axis = 1.8,
       xlim = c(-0.65,0.45),
       alim = c(-0.35,0.2))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits

# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 2.5, par("usr")[2], 3.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 3
rect(par("usr")[1], 5.5, par("usr")[2], 6.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 6
rect(par("usr")[1], 9.5, par("usr")[2], 10.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 10


text(-0.36, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label


title("Association of Childhood SES and DNAm Measures\nof Biological Aging Measured During Childhood in Venous Blood", line = 0, cex.main = 2.5)

# Add * significant results
for (n in 1:length(pvals)) {
  if (pvals[n] < 0.05) {
    mtext("*", side = 4, line = 1, cex = 3, las = 1, at = (length(pvals)-n+1))
  }
}
dev.off()
```


### Saliva
```{r}
# prepare data
data_ses3_sal <- data_ses %>%
  filter(SES_time3 == 1) %>%
  filter(Tissue_saliva == 1)

# associations per generation
# gen 1
gen1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen2 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_sal)

# gen 2
gen2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen3, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_sal)

# gen 3
gen3 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_gen1 + DNAm_gen2, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_sal)

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for generation-level analyses 
variance_distribution(data_ses3_sal,gen1) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses3_sal,gen1)

# associations of DNAm measures
## first gen
#horvath1
horvath1 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_sal)

#hannum
hannum <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_sal)

#horvath2
horvath2 <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_sal)

#pedbe
pedbe <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_horvath1, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_sal)

## second gen
#phenoage
phenoage <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_sal)

#grimage
grimage <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_sal)

## third gen
#dunedinpoam
poam <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_dunedinpace + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_sal)

#dunedinpace
pace <- rma.mv(Fisher_z, z_variance, mods = ~ DNAm_grimage + DNAm_horvath2 + DNAm_hannum + DNAm_phenoage + DNAm_horvath1 + DNAm_dunedinpoam + DNAm_pedbe, random = list(~ 1 | Effect_Size_ID, ~ 1 | Cohort_ID), data=data_ses3_sal)

#see percentages of how variance is distributed across levels for DNAm-level analyses (since the meta-analytic model is the same for all DNAm measures, running this once should be enough)
variance_distribution(data_ses3_sal,horvath1) 
#get significance levels for variance at levels 2 and 3
heterogeneity_analysis(data_ses3_sal,horvath1)
```

```{r}
#get estimates, lower and higher cis for graphs (full dataset)
ests <- c(coef(gen1)[1], coef(horvath1)[1], coef(hannum)[1], coef(horvath2)[1], coef(pedbe)[1], 
          coef(gen2)[1], coef(phenoage)[1], coef(grimage)[1], coef(gen3)[1], coef(poam)[1], coef(pace)[1])

lowcis <- c(gen1$ci.lb[1], horvath1$ci.lb[1], hannum$ci.lb[1], horvath2$ci.lb[1], pedbe$ci.lb[1],
            gen2$ci.lb[1], phenoage$ci.lb[1], grimage$ci.lb[1], gen3$ci.lb[1], poam$ci.lb[1], pace$ci.lb[1])

highcis <- c(gen1$ci.ub[1], horvath1$ci.ub[1], hannum$ci.ub[1], horvath2$ci.ub[1], pedbe$ci.ub[1],
            gen2$ci.ub[1], phenoage$ci.ub[1], grimage$ci.ub[1], gen3$ci.ub[1], poam$ci.ub[1], pace$ci.ub[1])

pvals <- c(gen1$pval[1], horvath1$pval[1], hannum$pval[1], horvath2$pval[1], pedbe$pval[1],
            gen2$pval[1], phenoage$pval[1], grimage$pval[1], gen3$pval[1], poam$pval[1], pace$pval[1])
  
es_no <- c(sum(data_ses3_sal$DNAm_gen1), sum(data_ses3_sal$DNAm_horvath1), sum(data_ses3_sal$DNAm_hannum), 
           sum(data_ses3_sal$DNAm_horvath2), sum(data_ses3_sal$DNAm_pedbe), sum(data_ses3_sal$DNAm_gen2), 
           sum(data_ses3_sal$DNAm_phenoage), sum(data_ses3_sal$DNAm_grimage), sum(data_ses3_sal$DNAm_gen3), 
           sum(data_ses3_sal$DNAm_dunedinpoam), sum(data_ses3_sal$DNAm_dunedinpace))


# convert all z's to r
ests <- convert_z2r(ests)
lowcis <- convert_z2r(lowcis)
highcis <- convert_z2r(highcis)


# Create a data frame for the forest plot
summary_effects <- data.frame(
  DNAm_measure = c("1st Generation", "Horvath Pan-Tissue", "Hannum", "Horvath Skin & Blood", "PedBE",
                   "2nd Generation", "PhenoAge", "GrimAge", "3rd Generation", "DunedinPoAm", "DunedinPACE"),           # DNAm_measure levels
  Estimate = ests,                     # Effect size estimates
  CI_lower = lowcis,                   # Lower bound of confidence interval
  CI_upper = highcis,                  # Upper bound of confidence interval
  ES_no = es_no                        # number of effect sizes
  )

png("childhood_saliva.png", width = 38, height = 25, units = "cm", res = 600)

par(mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 4))

# Create the forest plot for the summary effects

forest(summary_effects$Estimate, 
       ci.lb = summary_effects$CI_lower, 
       ci.ub = summary_effects$CI_upper, 
       slab = summary_effects$DNAm_measure,        # Labels for the forest plot (DNAm_measure levels)
       xlab = "Effect Size (Pearson's r)", 
       header = c("DNAm Measure"), 
       ilab = summary_effects$ES_no, ilab.xpos = -0.37,
       cex = 1.75,
       cex.lab = 2,
       cex.axis = 1.8,
       xlim = c(-0.65,0.45),
       alim = c(-0.35,0.2))                           # Adjust the x-axis limits

# Color
rect(par("usr")[1], 2.5, par("usr")[2], 3.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 3
rect(par("usr")[1], 5.5, par("usr")[2], 6.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 6
rect(par("usr")[1], 10.5, par("usr")[2], 11.5, border = NA, col= rgb(0.27, 0.55, 0.45, alpha=0.5))  # Shading for row 11


text(-0.36, length(summary_effects$DNAm_measure) + 2.1, "Nr. of Effect Sizes", cex = 1.75, font = 2)  # Adjust position of the label


title("Association of Childhood SES and DNAm Measures\nof Biological Aging Measured During Childhood in Saliva", line = 0, cex.main = 2.5)

# Add * significant results
for (n in 1:length(pvals)) {
  if (pvals[n] < 0.05) {
    mtext("*", side = 4, line = 1, cex = 3, las = 1, at = (length(pvals)-n+1))
  }
}
dev.off()
```
